races.race_name: Soil Association 80th Anniversary Conditions Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Love Is
runners.spotlight: 90,000euros yearling; Sioux Nation half-sister to four winners; picked up well from the back and was nicely on top at the finish in Windsor event (6f, good to firm); promising filly.
runners.comment: Picked up well en route to being nicely on top at the finish in Windsor event; promising

runners.name: Night In Vegas
runners.spotlight: 60,000gns yearling; Lucky Vega half-brother to five winners; got on top towards the finish in Ascot contest (6f, good) and looks a plausible Royal Ascot hope; respected.
runners.comment: Got on top towards the finish in Ascot contest and looks a plausible Royal Ascot hope

runners.name: Shadow King
runners.spotlight: Dark Angel colt; made all at Kempton (6f, AW) to step up on his Newmarket effort (5f); appears to face a stiffer task back on turf but may be capable of further progress.
runners.comment: AW winner who seems to face a stiffer task back on turf but may progress again
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Lazurite
runners.spotlight: Trainer rode this filly's dam to Listed 2yo success during his riding days; promising half-length second of eight, from the front, in Newmarket event (5f, good to firm) on 1,000 Guineas day; leading contender.
runners.comment: Promising second, from the front, at Newmarket on 1,000 Guineas day; leading contender

runners.name: Pershaada
runners.spotlight: Beaten favourite in a couple of 5f AW contests, nevertheless showing ability, last time second behind the subsequent Marygate (Listed) winner; may still do better and it remains too early to write her off.
runners.comment: Beaten favourite in two AW events, nevertheless showing ability; may still do better

runners.name: Fast Track
runners.spotlight: Attractively bred newcomer, by Blue Point out of a 6f 2yo winner (on debut; later Group placed; RPR 97) for her owner; interesting, especially if the market speaks positively.
runners.comment: Attractively bred, by Blue Point out of a 6f 2yo winner (on debut) for her owner

runners.name: Magic Effort
runners.spotlight: The offspring of two winners for her owner; prevailed only narrowly at the Newmarket Craven meeting (5f, good) but is open to progress and a penalty is partly offset by Mason Paetel's useful 5lb claim.
runners.comment: Penalty for narrow win at Newmarket is partly offset by Mason Paetel's useful claim

runners.name: Kentucky Rain
runners.spotlight: 18,000euros yearling; Lucky Vega half-sister to two winners, notably Son Of Corballis (5f 2yo including Listed; RPR 93); dam related to two UAE Oaks winners; check the betting.
runners.comment: 18,000euros yearling; Lucky Vega half-sister to two winners; check the betting

runners.name: Lola
runners.spotlight: 17,000gns foal, 42,000euros yearling; Caturra half-sister to three winners, notably King Cuan (Group-placed 6f/7f 2yo; RPR 112); dam 7f AW/7.5f turf scorer (85); market informative.
runners.comment: 42,000euros yearling; Caturra half-sister to three winners; market informative

runners.name: Targa
runners.spotlight: £62,000 yearling, £105,000 (breeze-up) 2yo; Blue Point half-sister to three winners, notably Showtime Mahomes (5f-7f including 2yo/AW; RPR 85); dam 5f AW 2yo scorer (72); likely type.
runners.comment: £105,000 (breeze-up) 2yo; by Blue Point out of 5f 2yo scorer; likely type

runners.name: Babzini
runners.spotlight: Showed some promise with third of six, best of the newcomers, in Salisbury conditions race (5f, good to firm) won by quite a useful sort (Listed placed since); not difficult to envisage her going well.
runners.comment: Showed some promise with third of six, best of the newcomers, in Salisbury conditions race

runners.name: Blushrose
runners.spotlight: 82,000euros yearling, £105,000 (breeze-up) 2yo; Persian Force half-sister to 6f AW winner Jorge Alvares (RPR 97); dam unraced, is granddaughter of 6f 2yo Group 2 scorer; major stable; good credentials.
runners.comment: £105,000 (breeze-up) 2yo; by Persian Force and descends from a 6f 2yo Group 2 winner

races.race_name: Track Radio Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: St Mawes
runners.spotlight: Usually held up; third in a 2yo maiden was his sole run on softer than good; can race freely but showed improved form when upped to 2m for wins at Kempton (AW) and Nottingham (good) in his final two runs last season, and he continued on his upward curve when a close, never-nearer third of seven over C&D (good) three weeks ago; retains potential.
runners.comment: Raced freely but close, never-nearer 3rd over C&D (good) on return was more improved form

runners.name: Caprelo
runners.spotlight: Close third on soft at Yarmouth (1m6f) last July was his best turf form; needs better given the improvement he's shown on Kempton AW (2m) in two of his three starts since, but he'd have a good chance if that form is transferred back to turf; won on 2024 reappearance; needs a close look.
runners.comment: 1st and 2nd in his last two Kempton AW starts; good chance if that form is transferred

runners.name: Bahadur
runners.spotlight: Unraced on softer than good when on turf; made immediate progress when switched to AW in the autumn, completing a hat-trick in a 1m4f novice and 1m4f/1m6f handicaps; perhaps in need of the race (albeit joint-favourite) on reappearance last month and fifth of 12 at Newmarket (1m6f) four weeks later was respectable, but settling him was an issue; suitability for a slog over 2m can therefore be questioned.
runners.comment: Fifth of 12 at Newmarket (1m6f) last time was respectable but settling him was an issue
runners.owner: J William Rowley & S P C Woods

runners.name: Sax Appeal
runners.spotlight: Very busy and he's had his share of wins, including four over 2m in 2025, but that's been mostly on AW which has dominated his career; best turf ratings were on last two outings but his turf form still needs improvement.
runners.comment: AW has dominated career; best turf ratings on last two outings but still needs better

runners.name: Give It To Me Oj
runners.spotlight: Acts on soft; can make the running; won six (four over hurdles, two on Flat) of seven starts in a golden spell last February-October; soundly beaten over hurdles on latest three outings (in tough races on last two) and unraced beyond 1m4f on Flat, but he can't be comfortably dismissed in this small field.
runners.comment: Can set pace; unraced beyond 1m4f on Flat but can't be comfortably dismissed in this field

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Fillies' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Venetia
runners.spotlight: Made all at Ascot (1m2f, good to firm) on handicap/seasonal debut, building on her 2yo efforts, and looks the type to progress further; bred to be suited by this longer distance; upped in class but receives plenty of weight all round; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Made all at Ascot on handicap/seasonal debut and looks the type to progress further

runners.name: Orionis
runners.spotlight: Yet to race on soft/heavy ground; steadily progressive last term, building up a solid record that featured three wins; good third at Newmarket final outing; cheekpieces worn the last twice are now removed; likely to go well.
runners.comment: Steadily progressive last term, building up a solid record that featured three wins

runners.name: Sibling Rivelry
runners.spotlight: Unraced on soft/heavy; appeared to have something in hand at Windsor (11.5f) last week on seasonal debut, taking turf record to 2-3 and overall strike-rate to 4-7; back up two grades but looks well treated under a penalty (future mark 1lb higher) and won over C&D last August; may improve further to boot; respected.
runners.comment: Well treated under a penalty (future mark 1lb higher) and may improve further to boot

runners.name: Amora Queen
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in the Lingfield Oaks Trial most recently but is somewhat interesting back down in grade, being a filly who has form figures of 112 when racing below Listed/Group level; ran well in Doncaster handicap (1m2f) the final time.
runners.comment: Has form figures of 112 when racing below Listed/Group level

runners.name: Caramay
runners.spotlight: Consistent AW handicapper and a seven-time winner in that sphere; only 1-9 on turf and appears to have a difficult task off current mark, especially in this grade, returned to this sphere.
runners.comment: Consistent AW handicapper; faces difficult task off current mark back on turf

runners.name: Charmaine
runners.spotlight: Three-time AW scorer; turf form features two good efforts but she's 0-4 in this sphere and was below par at Doncaster most recently; not the percentage call in this warmer contest.
runners.comment: Three-time AW scorer; below par returned to turf last time; this is harder

runners.name: Typical Woman
runners.spotlight: Ended her 2025 campaign with an August win but hasn't been in anything like the same form since returning to action and is the most exposed, as well as the oldest, contender in this field.
runners.comment: Has a doubt over current form and is the oldest and most exposed contender

runners.name: Quebella
runners.spotlight: Trained last year by Richard Hughes, winning on debut then holding her form well, including two creditable efforts at Goodwood; sold for 70,000gns since last run; the betting may help to ascertain expectations on stable/seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Held her form well last year for Richard Hughes; sold for 70,000gns since last run

runners.name: Miss Dolly Rocker
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner, including over C&D; absent since creditable second in last year's November Handicap at Doncaster on debut for new yard; hit with another rise in the weights but drops back in class and may remain competitive.
runners.comment: Absent since creditable second in last year's November Handicap on stable debut

races.race_name: Fitzdares Festival Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Boiling Point
runners.spotlight: Defied top weight in the 2025 Cambridgeshire (1m1f, good to firm) and holds a leading chance provided that smart handicap form is reproduced; hasn't transferred it back to Group level this term but he's respected on ratings and is a similar type to last year's winner of this race (for same connections).
runners.comment: Respected on ratings and is a similar type to last year's winner (for same connections)
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Haatem
runners.spotlight: Has form on all types of ground; smart performer whose strong CV includes wins in the Vintage Stakes at Goodwood as a 2yo and two Royal Ascot contests, more recently last year's Wolferton (1m2f Listed); best figures give him a major chance at the weights.
runners.comment: Smart performer whose strong CV features two Royal Ascot wins; major contender

runners.name: High Stock
runners.spotlight: Record of 0-5 since debut win (Wood Ditton) but he's useful and ran creditably in sole C&D attempt, namely fourth to Naqeeb in Listed event; could go well with Chester (Group 2) reappearance under his belt.
runners.comment: Record of 0-5 since debut win but he's useful and ran creditably in sole C&D attempt

runners.name: Ice Max
runners.spotlight: Won the Celebration Mile at Goodwood in 2024; creditable third, faring best of the runners who came off steady pace, in Group 2 at Chester (good) upped to 1m2f last time; unexposed at this distance and enters calculations despite carrying a 3lb penalty for Listed success (1m, soft) last autumn.
runners.comment: Creditable third in Chester Group 2 upped to 1m2f last time; unexposed at this trip
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Enfjaar
runners.spotlight: Unlucky second at Glorious Goodwood in 2024 when bidding for a notable 1m2f handicap double; best effort in a truncated 2025 campaign when fourth to Haatem in Listed grade at Royal Ascot; should be thereabouts, provided he's in top form after ten-month layoff.
runners.comment: Ties in with Haatem on 2025 Royal Ascot running; returns from ten-month layoff
runners.owner: T & Mrs L Standish & Mrs A Spence

runners.name: Involvement
runners.spotlight: Missed 2025; last two performances, namely success at Meydan (handicap) and third at Longchamp (Listed), show that he retains plenty of ability; however, has something to find back on home soil.
runners.comment: Retains plenty of ability but has something to find back on home soil

runners.name: La Botte
runners.spotlight: Unlucky second in last year's Britannia at Royal Ascot then caught the eye on reappearance; hasn't exactly lived up to expectations in two starts since but remains of interest assuming this new scenario (steps up from 1m) prompts a positive effect; too soon to give up on him.
runners.comment: His most promising runs are not forgotten and it's too soon to give up on him; new trip
runners.owner: Halo Racing N Greenhalgh J Degenhardt

runners.name: Naqeeb
runners.spotlight: Performed well twice over C&D last term but had the run of the race when belying his 33-1 odds for neck success at Listed level in September (good to soft) and looks no certainty to defy a 3lb penalty back here, unless things again fall his way.
runners.comment: Had the run of the race when 33-1 winner of similar event over C&D last September

runners.name: Certain Lad
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner down the years but 2025 figures suggest age may be catching up with him to some degree; hasn't won a domestic race since 2023 and it's an even longer time since he made a successful seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Age may be catching up with him; hasn't won a domestic race since 2023

races.race_name: Edward Bulmer Natural Paint Handicap

runners.name: Kisskodi
runners.spotlight: Unraced on soft/heavy; scored four times on turf last term and may resume his progress with creditable AW reappearance under his belt; combined record of 3-4 at Epsom and Brighton bodes well for this first visit to Goodwood, which is quite similar to those tracks; highly respected.
runners.comment: May resume progress with AW reappearance under his belt; should take well to Goodwood

runners.name: Crimson Spirit
runners.spotlight: Best form at 7f on good/firmer; completed a hat-trick last summer and ran creditably in big field at Newmarket in the first of his two starts this term; chance may depend on the actions of the weather, however.
runners.comment: Best form at 7f on good/firmer; chance may depend on the actions of the weather

runners.name: The Dragon King
runners.spotlight: Previously effective on turf but form dipped returned to this sphere last time and he still hasn't scored on grass since his 2yo days; admittedly on a workable mark provided he rebounds.
runners.comment: Form dipped returned to turf last time; hasn't scored in this sphere since his 2yo days

runners.name: Walson's Law
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 5yo who has gained both wins (7f/1m) on good to firm; grabbed third place on line over 6f for an encouraging reappearance effort and looks interesting back up in distance, with further improvement still a possibility.
runners.comment: Low-mileage 5yo who looks interesting back up in trip; further progress is still possible
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Lady Cobham

runners.name: Philanthropist
runners.spotlight: Took well to first-time cheekpieces at Yarmouth (7f, good to firm) returned to turf with AW reappearance under his belt, taking overall record to 3-13; upped in grade but may have more to offer in the retained headgear; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Took well to cheekpieces last time and may have more to offer in the retained headgear

runners.name: Oliver Show
runners.spotlight: Useful on his day but losing run is mounting up and two duck eggs this year leave a question mark over his current form; chance partly depends on how well he responds to the removal of usual cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Chance partly depends on how well he responds to removal of usual cheekpieces

runners.name: Shiplake
runners.spotlight: Record of 3-5 on AW includes two stylish wins over 7f at Southwell for new yard; unfavourable draw played a part in him failing to transfer the form back to turf in the Victoria Cup when admittedly taking record in this sphere to 0-6.
runners.comment: Unfavourable draw played a part in him failing to transfer AW form back to turf last time

runners.name: Morte Point
runners.spotlight: Produced form figures of 1211 once upped to 7f last term; has posted two duck eggs this season but raced further back than usual on reappearance and was unfavourably drawn in the Victoria Cup latest; may prove resurgent.
runners.comment: Pair of duck eggs this term don't tell the whole story; record of 1211 over 7f previously

runners.name: Al Arbeed
runners.spotlight: Has been unable to sustain early progress and is 0-7 since debut success; ran respectably on penultimate start and is on a reduced mark but chance of doubling his tally largely depends on the effects of first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Chance of doubling his tally largely depends on the effects of first-time headgear

runners.name: Mister Bluebird
runners.spotlight: Went close at Musselburgh (7f, good) last time, showing he retains full ability aged eight; dual winner at Goodwood and may remain competitive; gained last three wins on soft ground.
runners.comment: Latest effort shows he retains full ability aged eight; may remain competitive

runners.name: Fouette
runners.spotlight: Similar type to Shiplake, with still having to prove that best AW form can be transferred back to turf; not exactly solid on 2026 efforts anyway and doesn't look the percentage call in this competitive field.
runners.comment: Still needs to prove her best AW form can be transferred back to turf

runners.name: Lunario
runners.spotlight: Returns to his ideal sphere (turf) but brings a question mark over his current form, having trailed home last in both AW outings this year; possibly out of his depth to boot, having never won above Class 4.
runners.comment: Returns to his ideal sphere but brings a question mark over current form

runners.name: Solar Aclaim
runners.spotlight: Didn't seem beaten entirely by the 7.5f trip at Chester on seasonal debut but is still seeking a first success beyond sprint distances; not exactly guaranteed to take advantage of being 1lb below last winning mark.
runners.comment: Not beaten entirely by 7.5f trip on reappearance but is still seeking first win beyond 6f

runners.name: Metal Merchant
runners.spotlight: Showed notable handicap form in 2024 but has posted duck eggs in three runs since that campaign and needs to prove he retains ability; best watched on second run for new yard, unless the market suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: Three duck eggs since 2024; needs to prove he retains ability; second run for new yard

races.race_name: Sustainable Food Trust Handicap

runners.name: Canon's House
runners.spotlight: Did well last term, winning four times; went close at Beverley on penultimate start and paid for racing freely in cheekpieces a few days later; runs without the headgear this time and could go well.
runners.comment: Went close at Beverley on penultimate start; paid for racing freely in cheekpieces latest
runners.owner: M McHale, J Munroe, M Guy & D Walshe

runners.name: Nogo's Dream
runners.spotlight: Has registered two wins apiece on AW and turf; went close at Nottingham (5f, good) most recently; again receives weight all round and may well remain competitive; has bits of C&D form.
runners.comment: Went close at Nottingham last time; again gets weight all round; may remain competitive

runners.name: Fidelius
runners.spotlight: Record of 6-17 on AW includes two wins in a consistent 2026 campaign; not disgraced back on turf last time but finished only fifth of six and took record in this sphere to 0-9; others preferred.
runners.comment: Not disgraced back on turf last time but took his record in this sphere to 0-9

runners.name: Seven Questions
runners.spotlight: Acts on any ground; on a losing spell that goes back to Group 3 success two years ago but is on a very attractive mark and his Class 2 form this term includes a couple of respectable efforts; this combination of a drop in grade and assistance of Jamie Spencer (up for first time; regular jockey for the owner) may be the time to catch him.
runners.comment: On two-year losing spell but is on a very attractive mark and drops in class

runners.name: Dream Composer
runners.spotlight: Regained the winning thread at Ripon (5f, good) on stable/seasonal debut when 5lb lower and rider took off 7lb; this seems a harder task but he remains well treated on peak form and is a dual winner at Goodwood; enters calculations.
runners.comment: Won at Ripon on stable/seasonal debut and remains well treated on peak form

runners.name: Dyonisos
runners.spotlight: Ex-French 4yo who is 0-7 for current yard but has two pieces of encouraging form this year, namely placed efforts at Meydan and Newmarket on good ground (perhaps ideally suited by softer); below par last time but otherwise has possibilities.
runners.comment: 0-7 for current yard but has two pieces of encouraging form this year; possibilities

runners.name: Star Chorus
runners.spotlight: Prevailed in reapplied hood at Southwell (6f) last time, taking AW record to 4-13; needs to prove he can transfer the form back to turf; only 1-10 in this sphere and is 7lb higher than last attempt.
runners.comment: Needs to prove he can transfer AW form back to turf; mark has gone up in tandem

runners.name: Roach Power
runners.spotlight: Very productive in 2025, winning five times; hindered by wide draw at Chester most recently, otherwise has a good record over 5f; just needs to prove he can overcome current mark.
runners.comment: Hindered by wide draw last time, otherwise has a good record over 5f

runners.name: Forager
runners.spotlight: Lacks recent match practice, remains on career-high mark and won't have her apparent ideal ground (good to firm) unless the weather lends her a big hand; not the percentage call.
runners.comment: Lacks recent match practice and remains on career-high mark

runners.name: Glamorous Breeze
runners.spotlight: Ended last season in good form on AW; has done most of her winning on turf but looks weighted to the hilt on reappearance, being still on a career-high mark; seems unlikely to improve aged eight.
runners.comment: Weighted to the hilt on reappearance, being still on career-high mark

races.race_name: Newsells Park Stud Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Fashion's Fancy
runners.spotlight: Inexperience evident when 5l third of six at 10-1 behind odds-on stablemate on last month's debut at Kempton (1m3f, AW); from a classy family and could have lots more to offer for her leading yard; key player.
runners.comment: Inexperience evident when third on last month's debut; could have lots more to offer

runners.name: Ladykirk
runners.spotlight: Kept on for promising second on debut at Yarmouth (1m, good to soft) last October and reappeared with 8l fifth of ten in strong novice at Sandown (1m2f, good) four weeks ago; entered in the Irish Oaks; might not be far away.
runners.comment: Promise on both starts and this Irish Oaks entry might not be far away

runners.name: Tribal Queen
runners.spotlight: Third foal; Frankel half-sister to 1m2f/1m4f winner Spirited Style (including Listed); dam 1m/1m2f Group 1 winner; could have a big part to play on debut, especially if strong in the betting; leading trainer also saddles Kempton third Fashion's Fancy.
runners.comment: Well-bred Godolphin newcomer; leading trainer also saddles Kempton third Fashion's Fancy

runners.name: Shadida
runners.spotlight: In top hands and her dam was a Listed winner; she's one to watch in the betting on this second start but she was always behind when 20-1 for last month's debut at Nottingham (8.3f, good) and dramatic improvement is needed.
runners.comment: In top hands but always behind when 20-1 for last month's debut at Nottingham

runners.name: Ceinture d'Orion
runners.spotlight: 210,000euros yearling with an attractive pedigree; shaped as though she would improve for the run when fourth of seven at 9-2 on last month's debut at Wetherby (1m2f, good to firm) and she's one to consider.
runners.comment: 210,000euros yearling; shaped as though she would improve for debut run; one to consider

runners.name: Nochebuena
runners.spotlight: 13-2 when staying on for 4l fourth of eight on debut at Kempton (7f, AW) last December; her dam was a 1m/1m2f Group 1 winner in Peru and the step up in trip should be a firm plus for this Study Of Man filly; chance.
runners.comment: Stayed on for fourth on 7f AW debut last December; this longer trip should be a firm plus

races.race_name: Oakmere Homes Supporting Macmillan EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Jazz Queen
runners.spotlight: Improved upon last month's debut sixth at Newmarket (5f, good) when going close at Chester (5.5f good) a fortnight ago, shaping as though she would be suited by today's extra yardage; takes on some very interesting newcomers but sets a useful form standard for them to aim at.
runners.comment: Chester runner-up; takes on some very interesting newcomers but sets useful form standard

runners.name: Bint Minzaal
runners.spotlight: 155,000euros yearling; eighth foal; Minzaal half-sister to four winners including Je Zous (1m/9.4f including 2yo/Group 3), I'll Have Another (7f-1m6f including 2yo; RPR 102) and Bucky Larson (7f/1m; 100); dam 10.6f winner (81); one of two newcomers for yard; could go well on debut; check betting.
runners.comment: 155,000euros yearling with an attractive pedigree; one of two newcomers for the yard

runners.name: Harlequin Gift
runners.spotlight: 95,000gns yearling; third foal; Advertise sister to 6f 2yo/1m winner Shout (RPR 105), half-sister to 7.3f/1m winner Port Erin (83); dam French 7f/7.5f winner (89), half-sister to 6f Group 2 winner Overdose; one of two debutantes for the stable; no surprise to see a good run; betting may guide.
runners.comment: 95,000gns yearling; sister to 6f 2yo/1m winner Shout; no surprise to see good run on debut

runners.name: Esha'a
runners.spotlight: £190,000 yearling; third foal; dam 7f/1m winner (including Swedish Listed), half-sister to two French winners out of 1m winner; has travelled from Lambourn for her debut and trainer's last two 2yo runners here have both won.
runners.comment: £190,000 yearling; makes debut; trainer's last two 2yo runners here have both won

runners.name: Royal Message
runners.spotlight: Second foal; No Nay Never half-sister to 7f AW winner Champion Again Ole (RPR 86); dam 6f winner (including 2yo/Group 2), from talented family; likely to improve for this debut run but could give good account.
runners.comment: Dam 6f winner (including 2yo/Group 2); likely to improve for this debut but could run well
runners.owner: Lord Lloyd Webber & W S Farish

runners.name: Minzelle
runners.spotlight: 85,000euros foal, 450,000gns yearling; by Minzaal; third foal; closely related to winners Turn On The Jets (5f including 2yo; RPR 112) and Saratoga Special (5f Listed); dam 5f winner (103); represents powerful northern yard that has made another fine start with 2yos; firmly in calculations on debut.
runners.comment: 450,000gns yearling whose top northern trainer has made another fine start with his 2yos

runners.name: Profitability
runners.spotlight: By Profitable; third foal; dam 8.6f AW winner (RPR 70), half-sister to useful winners Real Force (5f-7.5f including 2yo) and Real Gain (1m-1m2f including 2yo); others newcomers make greater appeal on paper.
runners.comment: Third foal; dam 8.6f AW winner (RPR 70); others newcomers make greater appeal on paper
runners.owner: Peter Onslow & Mr & Mrs Gary Middlebrook

runners.name: Sea Venture
runners.spotlight: 70,000gns yearling (150,000gns vendor 2yo breeze-ups); fifth foal; Sea The Stars sister to winners Layaleena (7f 2yo/1m; RPR 93) and Beccara Rose (1m; 86); well-bred dam once-raced (52); trainer has already unearthed some useful 2yos this season and she's one to be interested in on debut.
runners.comment: Trainer has already unearthed some useful 2yos this year; one to be interested in on debut

runners.name: Trust In Far
runners.spotlight: 10,000euros yearling, £15,000 2yo breeze-ups; tenth foal; Blackbeard half-sister to seven winners notably Stone Of Destiny (5f-6f; RPR 109), Stone Tornado (1m Listed) and Illusionist (5f-6f including 2yo; 97); she may be best watched on debut.
runners.comment: £15,000 2yo breeze-ups; half-sister to seven winners but she may be best watched on debut

runners.name: Aurora Valkyrie
runners.spotlight: 18,000euros yearling; third foal; half-sister to 10.6f AW winner Lord Talbot (RPR 56); unraced dam closely related to 1m6f Listed winner Arya Tara, out of 1m2f winner; this Sottsass newcomer may prefer a longer trip.
runners.comment: Newcomer; Sottsass half-sister to 10.6f AW winner (RPR 56); she may prefer a longer trip

runners.name: Libertango
runners.spotlight: 130,000euros yearling, 400,000gns 2yo breeze-ups; first foal; dam unplaced 6f/1m 2yo in US, sister to 7f 2yo Group 3 winner Aktabantay, half-sister to winners Baydar (smart 1m-1m2f) and Reina Madre (French 7f Group 3), out of 1m2f Listed winner; Newmarket trainer is 5-14 (36%) with his 2yos here; this No Nay Never newcomer could play a leading role.
runners.comment: 400,000gns 2yo breeze-up buy; Newmarket trainer does well with his 2yos here

runners.name: Resemblance
runners.spotlight: £45,000 yearling who has an attractive pedigree and is open to improvement on this second start; however, she was well beaten at 16-1 at Ascot (5f, good) a fortnight ago and needs to be an entirely different proposition.
runners.comment: Well beaten at 16-1 at Ascot recently and needs to be an entirely different proposition

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Cecil Frail Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Sky Majesty
runners.spotlight: Listed/Group 3 wins at Naas last summer (6f, yielding/good) then unplaced in two Group 1s; underwhelming on recent return at Newmarket (6f, good to firm; behind two of these) but those who raced more prominently were favoured and she could improve for that run; versatile ground-wise; key player.
runners.comment: Listed/Group 3 winner last summer and she could improve for her recent return at Newmarket

runners.name: Rosy Affair
runners.spotlight: Listed winner at Pontefract (6f, good to firm) last August and followed up in Group 3 at Deauville (6f, soft) 13 days later; reappeared with near-miss in Listed race at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) and has strong claims.
runners.comment: Listed/Group 3 winner last August; near-miss in Listed race on reappearance; strong claims

runners.name: Magic Basma
runners.spotlight: 0-10 since winning on debut in August 2024 but she ran well in Listed races last August/September (various ground) and did likewise when fourth of nine on reappearance at Newmarket three weeks ago (6f, good to firm; Rosy Affair about 2l ahead in second); could be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Some solid form in defeat in Listed races and she could be thereabouts

runners.name: Half Sovereign
runners.spotlight: 5-7 in 2yo season, a campaign that culminated with Group 3 win at Chantilly (7f, very soft); the drop back in trip could be a positive but she's been well beaten on both starts this season, most recently in the French 1,000 Guineas; carries a 5lb penalty and a major turnaround is needed.
runners.comment: 7f Group 3 win in France last October but she's struggled there on both starts this season

runners.name: Lady Youmzain
runners.spotlight: Impressive at Beverley (5f, soft) last September on sole 2yo start and readily justified odds-on favouritism in four-runner Pontefract novice (6f, good) last month; this is a steep rise in grade but it remains to be seen where her limitations lie; she's one to consider.
runners.comment: 2-2; steep rise in grade today but it remains to be seen where her limitations lie

runners.name: Princess Petrol
runners.spotlight: Went close in Group 3 Sweet Solera at Newmarket (7f, good to firm) last August and again ran well when fourth of five in Group 2 at Longchamp (1m, soft) in September; however, well beaten in Group 3/Listed races (7f/6f, good) on her two starts this spring and has to get back on track.
runners.comment: Went close in Group 3 at Newmarket last August but well beaten on both starts this spring

runners.name: Figjam
runners.spotlight: Ran four times in last year's 2yo campaign and best form came over 6f on soft/heavy ground, with a Group 3 fourth at Ayr and a Listed second at Doncaster; not beaten far when seventh of 14 at 66-1 on reappearance in the Group 3 Fred Darling at Newbury (7f, good); each-way chance back on slow ground.
runners.comment: Not beaten far in the Fred Darling on reappearance; each-way chance back on slow ground

runners.name: Splish Splash
runners.spotlight: All five starts on good ground; easily made all in C&D maiden last October on third outing and third of 19 in Listed race at Newmarket (6f, good) a fortnight later; she's much better than she showed on her reappearance at Newbury but was heavily beaten; others arrive with more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Listed third at Newmarket last October but heavily beaten in Group 3 at Newbury on return

runners.name: Rhyme Dust
runners.spotlight: More than just not handling the track was amiss when she was last on her recent return at Newmarket (6f, good to firm); however, this lightly raced 4yo showed improved form when tackling soft ground at Ayr last September, finishing third in that 5.5f Listed race; not discounted back on slow ground.
runners.comment: Listed third at Ayr last September and she's not discounted back on slow ground

runners.name: Dandana
runners.spotlight: Won last summer on first two starts (6f, AW/good) before respectable fourth in the Group 2 Lowther at York (6f, good to firm); however, tailed off in Group 3 Firth of Clyde at Ayr (6f, soft) in September and she has something to prove on the forecast slow ground on this reappearance.
runners.comment: Fourth in Group 2 Lowther last August but tailed off in Ayr Group 3 on soft when last seen

runners.name: Lady Roxby
runners.spotlight: Steadily progressive in turf handicaps last season and reappeared with encouraging third at Kempton (6f, AW) last month; however, well beaten at Newmarket since and looks up against it today even if back to her best.
runners.comment: Solid profile in h'caps prior to a blip last time; up against it even if back to her best

runners.name: Coconut Cove
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the third attempt at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) last October and took another step forward when sixth of 19 in Listed race at Newmarket later that month (6f, good; unraced on softer); with a good trainer and she's open to further improvement, but it's very much necessary today.
runners.comment: Progressive 2yo last year; open to further improvement but it's very much necessary today

runners.name: Trust Amy
runners.spotlight: Won Chester novice (6f, good to soft) last September on fourth run but came up short in Listed race and two nurseries subsequently; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Came up short in Listed race and two nurseries towards end of last season; hard to fancy

runners.name: Paris Babe
runners.spotlight: Two-time winner in France, the latest last November; however, others have stronger form and she hasn't been at the top of her game this spring on her first two runs in Britain.
runners.comment: Two-time winner in France but not at the top of her game in Britain this spring

runners.name: Angel Shared
runners.spotlight: Completed 6f Newmarket handicap hat-trick last summer (good to firm); kept on well for fifth of 14 in Listed race at Bath (5f, good) last month and close third in handicap there (5f, good) latest; has run well on heavy ground here; improvement needed but the return to 6f can suit; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Kept on for fifth of 14 in Bath Listed race (5f); the return to 6f can suit; not ruled out

runners.name: Powdering
runners.spotlight: Admirable 6yo who has won eight of her 52 starts (including on soft); however, she goes up in grade this afternoon and would be a shock winner.
runners.comment: Exposed 6yo who goes up in grade this afternoon and would be a shock winner

races.race_name: Oakmere Homes Together For Macmillan EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Pat's Cash
runners.spotlight: 19,000gns foal, 55,000gns yearling; sixth foal; Perfect Power half-brother to US 5.5f-6.5f winner Philipsburg; dam unraced half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 2 winner Shadn, out of 7f Listed winner; trainer is enjoying an excellent spring with his 2yos and this one could play a leading role on debut.
runners.comment: Trainer is enjoying an excellent spring with his 2yos; could play a leading role on debut

runners.name: Tribal Appearance
runners.spotlight: 4-5 favourite when no match for the winner in second of five on last Wednesday's debut at Bath (5.7f, firm); there are mixed messages in his pedigree regarding the suitability of today's step up in trip but he's open to improvement and is entitled to respect.
runners.comment: Second on last week's debut (5.7f); mixed messages in pedigree regarding this sort of trip

runners.name: Time For The Moon
runners.spotlight: 60,000gns yearling; 11th foal; Sea The Moon brother to 1m4f Australian Group 1 winner Durston, half-brother to four winners including Cubanita (1m-1m4f including 2yo/Group 3) and Aquarium (7f-1m2f; RPR 109); there's plenty of potential in his pedigree and he could be a contender on debut.
runners.comment: There's plenty of potential in his pedigree and he could be a contender on debut

runners.name: The Bookkeeper
runners.spotlight: 10-1 and hampered leaving the stalls when fourth of six, beaten just over 5l, on last Thursday's debut at Salisbury (5f, good); he made some late headway and, with his dam being a 7f 2yo winner, he's open to improvement at this longer trip; interesting, all the more so if well backed.
runners.comment: Late headway on last week's debut over 5f at Salisbury and the step up to 7f may well suit

races.race_name: Oakmere Homes Racing For Macmillan Handicap

runners.name: Hill O Rue
runners.spotlight: Won handicaps last season when always prominent at Carlisle (1m1f, good to soft) in May and Beverley (1m2f, soft) in September; no show when 14-1 for reappearance one month ago, his first race on good to firm, but it's not hard to think he will be a lot more potent today.
runners.comment: The ground was softer than good for his 1m1f/1m2f wins last term; one to consider
runners.owner: Gordon Bulloch & S Hardcastle D Szepler

runners.name: Imperial Trooper
runners.spotlight: Placed on three of five starts this year; wide draw no help at Chester two weeks ago when he had his first crack at 1m4f (tailed off); reverts to 1m2f and probably okay on the ground, so he's considered each-way.
runners.comment: Reverts to 1m2f and probably okay on the ground, so he's considered each-way

runners.name: Bellasio
runners.spotlight: In good form last May-July, winning at Carlisle (1m, soft) and runner-up on four occasions, including there at 1m1f on soft; never dangerous on reappearance 15 days ago, which also happened at the same juncture last term before a good run next time; player if he can stay 1m2f, which isn't nailed on.
runners.comment: In good form last May-July; player if he can stay 1m2f, which isn't nailed on

runners.name: Pol Roger
runners.spotlight: Lost his way in the autumn; has a favourable mark as a result and reappearance six weeks ago (without usual cheekpieces, which now return) was probably a step back up the ladder, but there's been no win for exactly two years.
runners.comment: Reappearance was probably a step back up the ladder; lacks any win for exactly two years

runners.name: Everyoneknowsadave
runners.spotlight: All three wins were on AW, at up to 1m3f; best turf efforts when placed at this track last August/September but his three AW runs this spring were rather muted; has career-low mark but needs to perk up.
runners.comment: Best turf efforts when placed here but his three AW runs this spring were rather muted

runners.name: Joseph
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in February, at the 16th attempt, and he narrowly justified favouritism last month as well; both of those races were over 1m2f but on Lingfield AW and he's accomplished little in his three turf races, the latest on penultimate start; others therefore look more solid.
runners.comment: Two 1m2f wins on Lingfield AW this year but he accomplished little in his three turf races

runners.name: Blue Wonder
runners.spotlight: Unraced on softer than good; struggled badly in her four handicaps last season but reappeared with a closer finish when fourth of ten at Lingfield (1m2f, AW; 16-1) late last month; needs to build on that.
runners.comment: Struggled badly in four handicaps last term but reappeared with a closer finish (1m2f, AW)

runners.name: Red Derek
runners.spotlight: 10yo who's 2-55, with last win in 2022; well handicapped on some 1m6f form last term and probably needed recent run, but whether he can be competitive at this trip nowadays remains to be seen.
runners.comment: Veteran who is only 2-55 (last win 2022); well handicapped on some 1m6f form last season

runners.name: Golspie
runners.spotlight: 1m2f wins last year at Pontefract (good) in June and Newcastle (AW) in December; pretty reliable and second of 14 at Doncaster (1m2f, good to soft) on return to turf in March showed again that he should be seriously involved.
runners.comment: Pretty reliable; second of 14 at Doncaster (1m2f, good to soft) in March last time

runners.name: Dragonflame
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden who ended last term with two poor shows, with blinkers given a go on final outing; subsequently left Brian Meehan for 15,000gns in October; has a piece of 1m2f form, but below form on his only start on softer than good and non-runner for old yard when the ground was testing.
runners.comment: Nine-race maiden who ended last term with two poor shows; left Brian Meehan for 15,000gns

runners.name: Give Me The Night
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden who has not lived up to initial promise; he's now 10lb lower than his opening mark but has not shown enough this term; moves up from 1m and there is stamina in the family.
runners.comment: Eight-race maiden who's not lived up to initial promise; up from 1m and stamina in family

runners.name: Lesrico
runners.spotlight: Ninth start when off the mark in game fashion from the front at Doncaster (1m2f, soft; 6lb higher today) in October under Ben Ffrench Davis; out of the handicap when appearing to run well 11 days later (1m2f, good) and he needs a second look on this reappearance.
runners.comment: Won from front at Doncaster (1m2f, soft; 6lb higher today) in October; needs a second look

runners.name: Norflondonforever
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 5yo who took a big step forward in first-time tongue-tie when landing a 1m handicap on Kempton AW last April by a head; that win was gained with a late challenge (sole attempt at 1m2f was debut) but he's been absent since, which probably poses the biggest question.
runners.comment: 5 races; first tongue-tie when winning at Kempton (1m, AW) last April by a head; off since

races.race_name: racingtv.com Handicap

runners.name: Bnaider
runners.spotlight: Third at Newbury (1m, soft) last October was his best form as 2yo and there was promise in his never-dangerous but staying-on fourth of six on handicap/seasonal debut at Sandown (1m2f, good; 6-1 from 8-1) four weeks ago; a big run is probably on its way.
runners.comment: Big run is probably on its way after a promising 1m2f fourth at Sandown on handicap debut

runners.name: Oathbound
runners.spotlight: Fifth of ten at Newbury (1m, soft) in October on debut but he's not lived up to that promise in his two races since; admittedly 16-1 for last month's Wetherby reappearance (1m, good to firm) behind an odds-on stablemate; this is also a new trip for handicap debut, representing a powerful yard.
runners.comment: Best effort on debut but this is a new trip for handicap debut; with a powerful yard

runners.name: Dalamara
runners.spotlight: Finished well when scoring on handicap debut at Newcastle (1m, AW) in December; ran creditably for minor honours in her two starts this season, over 1m on good and good to firm, after which 1m2f now looks in order (backed up by pedigree); place claims.
runners.comment: Minor honours this term and 1m2f now looks in order (backed up by pedigree); place claims

runners.name: Kokoluna
runners.spotlight: Big step forward on handicap debut when scoring at Nottingham (1m, good) last month; set a steady pace when trying 1m2f at Goodwood (good) three weeks ago, resulting in a creditable fourth of seven but she clearly needs to build on that.
runners.comment: Won at Nottingham (1m, good); set a steady pace when trying 1m2f at Goodwood (good; 4th)

runners.name: Impossible Mission
runners.spotlight: Handicap debut winner at Wolverhampton (upped to 9.4f, AW) in November but ran poorly both starts since, returned to turf when tailed off at Newmarket (1m2f, good; 40-1) latest; down in class but caution advised.
runners.comment: Returned to turf when tailed off at Newmarket (1m2f, good; 40-1) latest; caution advised

runners.name: Ibn Altheeb
runners.spotlight: Seven races last year for David Loughnane and sole win in a Wolverhampton nursery (8.6f, AW) in October in first-time cheekpieces, which were kept when never dangerous in his two starts since; latest was stable/seasonal debut (25-1) and maybe he needed that run, but others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Maybe he needed his stable/seasonal debut (25-1) but others have more pressing claims

runners.name: Lady Hornblower
runners.spotlight: 7.4f Beverley maiden winner (good to firm; acts on soft) from four 2yo starts; faded when favourite for handicap/seasonal debut there in April and her Doncaster 1m run three weeks ago was a good deal worse, so she's best watched for the time being.
runners.comment: Faded when favourite for handicap/seasonal debut and latest run was a good deal worse

runners.name: Central Command
runners.spotlight: Well held in three 7f AW runs late last year but second favourite with the visor enlisted for handicap/seasonal debut at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) three weeks ago, coming from towards rear to win comfortably in the end; up 7lb and makes turf debut but he has to be in serious calculations.
runners.comment: Up 7lb for this turf debut but there was plenty to like about his Newcastle win (1m2f, AW)

runners.name: Ephron
runners.spotlight: 11-2 when seen in a very new light for handicap/seasonal debut at Redcar (1m, good to firm) three weeks ago, coming from rear to win a nine-runner race by nearly 2l going away; 7lb higher but there was plenty of encouragement for 1m2f in that performance; one to consider.
runners.comment: Won handicap/seasonal debut at Redcar (1m, good to firm) with a strong finish; up 7lb
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Lxvi And P Demilow

runners.name: Rumbustious
runners.spotlight: Staying-on third of six in maiden at Chelmsford (1m2f, AW) in March was best effort; while not disgraced when fourth of nine on handicap/turf debut at Doncaster (1m2f, good to firm) four weeks ago, much better is needed.
runners.comment: While not disgraced on handicap/turf debut (1m2f, good to firm), much better is needed

runners.name: Carwyn
runners.spotlight: Showed ability in two of his three maiden races in Ireland but well beaten on handicap debut there (1m) in October and a remote last of eight at Chester (1m4f, good; 80-1) on British debut 15 days ago; cheekpieces (worn for his last two Irish starts) return.
runners.comment: Remote last of eight at Chester (1m4f, good; 80-1) on British debut 15 days ago

runners.name: Lavender Gold
runners.spotlight: Placed twice in the mud last September (made most on second occasion) before running on but never getting in a serious blow when 13-8 favourite for handicap debut at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) in November, still looking capable of better; had wind and gelding operations this spring; one to check out for a yard which had two notable well-backed winners last week.
runners.comment: Looked capable of better as 2yo; wind and gelding operations this spring; one to check out
runners.owner: All For One Racing Ix & Chris Wright

races.race_name: Oakmere Homes Green Ribbon Handicap

runners.name: Alkumatic Jo Jo
runners.spotlight: Promising third at Bath (1m, soft) last October on second start and runner-up in Newcastle nursery (7f, AW) next time; she hasn't shone on her two runs this spring (1m/1m2f, good/good to firm) but the drop back in trip and return to slower ground may help.
runners.comment: Hasn't shone this spring but the drop back in trip and return to slower ground may help

runners.name: Jungle Knight
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden who is yet to finish better than fourth but there were hints of promise in 7f nurseries here (good/good to soft; unraced on softer) last September and the way he kept on when mid-division on his recent reappearance at Southwell (7f, AW) suggests 1m could be a good fit.
runners.comment: 0-6 at up to 7f and yet to finish better than fourth but has hinted 1m will be a good fit

runners.name: Sovereign Bay
runners.spotlight: All three starts over 1m on AW; runner-up on debut at Newcastle in February; two backward steps have followed but he could be a contender if tapping back into his initial promise on this handicap/turf debut.
runners.comment: Two backward steps since going close on debut; chance if tapping back into initial promise

runners.name: Kiss For An Angel
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way on reappearance at Doncaster (1m2f, good to firm) four weeks ago and it's hard to be confident on the back of that; however, he may have needed the run and he was a close second of five at Redcar (1m, good) last November in his sole nursery; a case can be made on that form.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way on reappearance but may have needed it; went close in his sole nursery

runners.name: Hambelton
runners.spotlight: 0-5 and yet to pose a serious threat but he ran fairly well from the front when fifth of 13 in a handicap at the Curragh (1m, soft) last month; he's travelled from Ireland to race today and could be in the shake-up.
runners.comment: Ran okay when fifth of 13 at the Curragh and this Irish raider could be in the shake-up

runners.name: Sagremor
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden who tackles 1m for the first time and could do with settling better, but the first-time hood could help in that regard and he kept on when fifth of 11 at Lingfield (7f, AW) last time; effective on soft/heavy ground last year and he might be in with a chance.
runners.comment: The first-time hood could help him settle; no ground issues; might be in with a chance

runners.name: Quick Turn
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the seventh attempt with an improved performance at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) in March; did it nicely on that occasion but he was soundly beaten in mid-division at Kempton (1m, AW) on Wednesday.
runners.comment: Won nicely at Wolverhampton in March; soundly beaten in midfield at Kempton on Wednesday

runners.name: Senor Campanaro
runners.spotlight: Reared at the start and couldn't get into it at Newmarket (7f, good) last month when back from a break; won a four-runner nursery at Newcastle (7f, AW) in December on his first run since being gelded and he drops back in grade today; could have a part to play.
runners.comment: No threat at Newmarket latest after a break but could have part to play back down in grade

runners.name: Blue Mantle
runners.spotlight: Disappointing favourite in Southwell novice (7f, AW) last month but short-headed in Newcastle maiden (7f, AW) previously; bred to be suited by this step up in trip and he's not ruled out on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Disappointing favourite last time but not ruled out given previous promise; handicap debut

runners.name: Yorkshire Queen
runners.spotlight: She ran okay when fourth of 11 behind a comfortable winner at Southwell (7f, AW) a fortnight ago on reappearance; however, this six-race maiden is starting to look exposed and she can be headstrong, which is a concern on this first crack at 1m.
runners.comment: Six-race maiden who is starting to look exposed; can be headstrong and goes up in trip

runners.name: Claytons Kolatonic
runners.spotlight: Made debut in February and his three starts have been on AW; improved form when fourth of 13 at 200-1 over 1m at Kempton last month, behind three useful opponents; one to consider on handicap debut.
runners.comment: 200-1 fourth at Kempton last month and he's one to consider on this handicap debut

runners.name: Paroda Diva
runners.spotlight: She's raced 13 times and won just once (last May); also unproven on slow ground; however, she kept on galloping when sixth of 11 over 7f at Newmarket three weeks ago and this first crack at 1m is worth a go.
runners.comment: 13 races and has won just once (last May) but this first crack at 1m is worth a go

races.race_name: Download The Fairplay App Handicap (Bath Summer Stayers' Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Basilette
runners.spotlight: Bumper/hurdles winner who's had another crack at the Flat on last two outings and scored here (11.5f, firm) nine days ago, leading over 1f out for a 5l win in six-runner race; penalty leaves her 3lb well-in compared to future handicaps and she's one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Won here (11.5f, firm) nine days ago, leading over 1f out for a 5l win in six-runner race

runners.name: Zooks
runners.spotlight: 37 starts, with her only wins coming last year on AW in February and at Ffos Las (1m6f, good) in July; went off the boil this January-March and no revival 15 days ago, after a break, but she'd be a major player if back on song.
runners.comment: Off the boil January-March and no revival 15 days ago; a major player if back on song

runners.name: Two Plus Two
runners.spotlight: Won on fast ground last May at Salisbury (1m6f) and Bath (11.5f); tailed off later that month but his recent reappearance here was a step back in the right direction; each-way claims if he's come on from that run.
runners.comment: Won twice on fast ground last May; recent reappearance was a step back in right direction

runners.name: Assured
runners.spotlight: Has not come up to scratch over 1m4f-2m1f in her four handicaps (trained last year by Marcus Tregoning, 2026 reappearance with this yard) and two of those were at this track, but she has fallen 9lb in the process.
runners.comment: Has not come up to scratch at 1m4f-2m1f in her four handicaps, first three for M Tregoning

runners.name: Captain Brett
runners.spotlight: 1m6f seemed the best fit last year, including for a win at Yarmouth (good) in June and also when first past the post at this track (firm; demoted after bumping) in July; perhaps in need of the race when last of five on return four weeks ago and he needs a second look back here.
runners.comment: 1m6f seemed the best fit last year; perhaps in need of the race on return four weeks ago

runners.name: Buck Barrow
runners.spotlight: Sole win (19 starts) was over 1m2f at Ffos Las last June; has the same mark today, having finished no better than sixth in his four races since, two of them this season; raced freely when behind here on his only attempt beyond 1m4f (1m5f last September) so there are enough doubts.
runners.comment: Below form lately; raced freely when behind here on sole go beyond 1m4f (1m5f last year)

runners.name: Everest
runners.spotlight: 17 races, finishing well in a 1m4f classified at Lingfield (AW) in March for her only win; second there off today's mark at same track (2m) last time was her best form under Flat rules and she has some turf form in the bumper and hurdle spheres; not entirely ruled out on Flat turf debut.
runners.comment: AW classified winner who  has some turf form in the bumper and hurdle spheres
runners.owner: Alan Jamieson Site Services & G L Moore

runners.name: Big Win
runners.spotlight: Upped in trip and 11-10 favourite when weakening into third of six on handicap debut at Southwell (2m, AW) in September but that is the best form from his five starts; has otherwise not gone beyond 1m4f (tried in cheekpieces on final outing) so today's new trip might offer hope on this first run since 18,000gns sale out of Harry Charlton's last October.
runners.comment: New trip might offer hope on first run since 18,000gns sale (left H Charlton) in October

runners.name: Man Of The Sea
runners.spotlight: Veteran whose two 2025 wins were at this track over 1m6f and 1m5f; placed twice over hurdles last November but last week's run in that sphere (after a Flat reappearance here) was disappointing and dents his appeal.
runners.comment: Veteran whose two 2025 wins were at this track; last week's hurdles run was disappointing

runners.name: Cloudy Rose
runners.spotlight: Had 1m4f and 1m6f wins on consecutive starts last summer and she's now 2lb below her last winning mark; following two respectable shows over 1m4f this term, she is one to take seriously on this return to 1m6f.
runners.comment: 2lb below last winning mark; two respectable shows this term; one to take seriously

races.race_name: MJ Church Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B-D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Madrisa
runners.spotlight: By Decorated Knight; first foal of an unraced half-sister to dual 1m2f Group 3 winner Extra Elusive and the yard's Group 2 Gimcrack winner Ajaya; 10-1 and blinkered for debut in 12-runner maiden at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) late last month but she was always close up and kept on stoutly to lead in the final strides; the clear form pick in this field and should improve.
runners.comment: Blinkered for Lingfield debut (1m2f, AW) but won; the clear form pick and should improve

runners.name: Supplicate
runners.spotlight: Showed promise on debut last October and justified 5-6 favouritism in 12-runner novice at Lingfield (again 1m on AW) in December; the latter was in workmanlike style but she's bred more for this trip and is open to further improvement, which is needed.
runners.comment: 5-6 for workmanlike novice win at Lingfield (1m, AW) in December; bred more for this trip

runners.name: Eloquencia
runners.spotlight: Modest form in a small field at Newmarket (7f, good) in April but second of eight to an impressive red-hot favourite in novice at Kempton (1m3f, AW) three weeks later; similar form to most of these and could improve again.
runners.comment: 2nd of eight to impressive red-hot favourite in a novice at Kempton (1m3f, AW) 16 days ago

runners.name: Clarissa Eclipse
runners.spotlight: Interesting on pedigree but she showed little when 25-1 in a five-runner novice at Yarmouth (11.5f, good to firm) one month ago.
runners.comment: Showed little when 25-1 in a novice at Yarmouth (11.5f, good to firm) one month ago

runners.name: Last Flight
runners.spotlight: 50-1 when never-dangerous seventh of 12 in maiden at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) last month, which was better than her December debut but winner Madrisa was 8l ahead at Lingfield and reopposes; major work to do despite revised terms.
runners.comment: Madrisa was 8l ahead at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) last time and reopposes; major work to do

runners.name: Spinney
runners.spotlight: Dam useful at 1m2f-1m4f but this daughter was a 1,000gns yearling and finished behind two of these rivals when 12-1 in a maiden at Lingfield (1m2f, AW).
runners.comment: Finished behind two of these rivals when 12-1 in a maiden at Lingfield (1m2f, AW

runners.name: West Hill Rosie
runners.spotlight: No worthwhile form in a bumper, a 1m novice or when behind three of today's rivals in a 1m2f maiden.
runners.comment: No worthwhile form in a bumper, a 1m novice or a 1m2f maiden

races.race_name: Wiltshire And Bath Air Ambulance Raceday 15th July British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Jaan Ki Tukri
runners.spotlight: Third of nine in a maiden at Newbury (5f, good) 16 days before her second of seven in a novice at Newmarket (5f, good to firm); raced front rank on both occasions and sets a high standard on the latter form.
runners.comment: Raced front rank when third at Newbury and second at Newmarket; latter sets high standard

runners.name: Paper View
runners.spotlight: Unable to justify favouritism over C&D (good) on debut; fifth of ten in maiden at Chester (5.5f, good) over four weeks later was better form, showing plenty of speed in the face of the widest draw; bold show seems likely but Jaan Ki Tikri looks a tough rival.
runners.comment: 5th of ten at Chester (5,5f, good) latest, showing plenty of speed in face of widest draw

runners.name: Minkaas
runners.spotlight: 80,000euros yearling, £140,000 breeze-up 2yo; third foal; Minzaal half-sister to 5.5f/6f winner Spiritualism (including 2yo; RPR 74); dam 7f winner (68), half-sister to 7f 2yo Group 2 winner/1,000 Guineas runner-up Lucida, out of half-sister to Breeders' Cup Turf winner English Channel; first 2yo of the season for her stable and she needs to be good.
runners.comment: 80,000euros yearling, £140,000 breeze-up 2yo by Minzaal; needs to be good in this field

runners.name: Flight Signal
runners.spotlight: 82,000gns foal, £180,000 breeze-up 2yo; fourth foal; Mehmas half-brother to 6f/7f winner Thurso (RPR 83); dam 5f winner (including Listed; 95), half-sister to a useful 5f/5.5f winner and out of Italian 5f Listed winner; bred for speed and the stable's 2yos have already made an impact this term.
runners.comment: £180,000 breeze-up 2yo; bred for speed and the stable's 2yos have made an impact this term

runners.name: On The Queue Tee
runners.spotlight: Dam US 5.5f turf 2yo winner; this daughter made most when third of six in a maiden at Salisbury (5f, good) eight days ago, despite starting 80-1, but two others in today's race have shown more.
runners.comment: Made most when third of six at Salisbury (5f, good) but others in this have shown more

races.race_name: Prix De Charlotte Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Silver Wraith
runners.spotlight: Scored here in (good to soft) in her final 3yo run and she started this campaign with another brave win over C&D (firm) last month; that took her Bath record to 2-2 and she's remains feasibly treated off only 2lb higher; key player.
runners.comment: Scored here in final 3yo run and she started this season with another C&D win; key player

runners.name: Moe's Legacy
runners.spotlight: Triple course winner who scored at Wolverhampton in March and she's gone close back at this track (5f/5,6f, firm) in last two runs; only 1lb higher than for her runner-up effort three weeks ago and she should make another bold bid.
runners.comment: Triple course winner who has finished close up here in her last two runs; respected

runners.name: Toolatetonegotiate
runners.spotlight: All three wins have been on AW but she's been knocking on door here (5f, firm/good) in last two starts; nudged up 2lb but she's been as good as ever in her last two runs and should be in the thick of things again.
runners.comment: All three wins have been on AW but she's been knocking on the door here in last two starts

runners.name: Cuban Lady
runners.spotlight: Clearcut winner of a Windsor novice (6f, good to firm) last June; finished close up in three of her four handicaps last season but she reappeared with a 12l defeat at Newmarket (100-30) three weeks ago; that was in a Class 3 after six months off but she needs to bounce back after that disappointing run.
runners.comment: Disappointing on Newmarket reappearance and she needs to bounce back near best

runners.name: Queue Dos
runners.spotlight: All four wins have been on AW and she's 0-11 on turf; finished placed a few times on good or quicker ground last summer but she's been quiet in both runs this season and others are more persuasive.
runners.comment: Four-time AW winner but she's 0-11 on turf and has been quiet in both runs this season

runners.name: Seraphim Angel
runners.spotlight: Triple turf winner who scored at Windsor (6f, good to firm) last July in her final run for Tom Dascombe; only 1lb higher than for that success but she's been out of sorts in two runs for new yard this spring and needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Triple turf winner but she's been out of sorts in two runs for new yard this season

races.race_name: Blackmore Build And Design Handicap

runners.name: Who Is Alice
runners.spotlight: Won a Chepstow maiden (7f, good to firm) for Jack Channon last July but she's not gone on from that and was beaten 13l on recent stable debut at Ascot (6f, good); drops back in grade off a reduced mark but record is now 1-9 and others are more convincing.
runners.comment: Won a 7f maiden last summer but she's not gone on from that and is now 1-9

runners.name: Our Guy
runners.spotlight: Improved form dropped to 6f on handicap debut when scoring under a prominent ride at Windsor (good to firm) 18 days ago; that was a narrow success at 33-1 but he's only 2lb higher here and is still unexposed after only four starts; respected.
runners.comment: Improved form dropped to 6f when winning on handicap debut at Windsor; respected up 2lb

runners.name: Luna Beaux
runners.spotlight: Dual Tapeta winner for new yard this year but she's not easy to predict and her form has slumped in last two starts; has bit to prove again and she's not finished closer than fifth in six turf runs.
runners.comment: Dual AW winner but she was a long way below form on Polytrack last time; down the list

runners.name: Too Darn Good
runners.spotlight: Didn't progress in six 2yo runs for Charlie Hills and was only sixth of ten when favourite on recent stable debut at Windsor (5f, good to firm); may have needed that run but he needs to raise his game back up in trip.
runners.comment: Seven-race maiden and he needs to raise his game back up in trip
runners.owner: K Hunter, D Leon And Hunscote Stud Ltd

runners.name: Beach Partee
runners.spotlight: Three wins for William Knight last autumn, but he's finished last in three runs for new yard this spring and latest was at Lingfield (5f, good) on Monday; has plenty to prove and he needs a major revival.
runners.comment: Has failed to beat a rival in three runs for current yard and he needs a major revival

runners.name: Escape Plan
runners.spotlight: Made it second-time lucky in handicaps when scoring at Kempton (6f, AW) in March but he couldn't make an impact at Windsor (6f, good to firm) 18 days ago; still lightly raced but he needs to resume his progress to defy this mark.
runners.comment: Still lightly raced but he needs to resume his progress to defy this mark

runners.name: Guernsey Lady
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in a 5f Lingfield novice (good) last May; reached the frame in a couple of handicaps since but she's been hard to predict and was last of seven in her final run (6f, AW) last year; record is now 1-11 and comes with risks attached on her return; visor worn last time is removed.
runners.comment: Inconsistent filly who has a record of 1-11; comes with risks attached on return

runners.name: Control Room
runners.spotlight: Unexposed filly who made all when landing a Catterick maiden (5f, soft; 7-2) last October; that was a very narrow success but she looks on a fair opening mark and should have more to offer as a 3yo; respected on handicap/seasonal debut
runners.comment: Catterick winner who is still unexposed and needs a close look on handicap/seasonal debut

runners.name: Double Naughty
runners.spotlight: Promising third in a soft-ground nursery at Thirsk (6f, soft) last September but she was well held here (5f, heavy) in final 2yo run; still lightly raced but she left Richard Hannon for 6,000gns in November and has bit to prove on her stable debut.
runners.comment: Five-race maiden who left Richard Hannon for 6,000gns in November; bit to prove on return

runners.name: Gelato
runners.spotlight: No progress when down the field in six runs in Ireland last season including three sprint handicaps; resumes off a reduced mark but she needs a transformation for new yard; blinkers removed.
runners.comment: Down the field in six runs in Ireland last season and needs a transformation for new yard

races.race_name: Blackmore Building Contractors Handicap (Bath Summer Sprint Series Qualifier) (Div I)

runners.name: Zambezi Diamond
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 5yo who returned after 485 days off with a front-running fourth of 13 in a Southwell maiden (6f, AW; 66-1) last month; carried her head awkwardly in the closing stages but that was a promising effort and she needs a close look back in a handicap.
runners.comment: Unexposed 5yo who showed promise on her comeback last month; in the mix back in a handicap

runners.name: Sisters In The Sky
runners.spotlight: Acts of firm and heavy ground; triple course winner who scored at Wolverhampton (6f, Tapeta) in January but he's been quiet there in last three runs; has bit to prove and needs to rediscover his spark back on turf.
runners.comment: Triple course winner but he needs to rediscover his spark back on turf

runners.name: Legendsoftheland
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 4yo; record of 3212 since wind/gelding operations, opening his account in a classified event at Wolverhampton (6f) before a creditable effort in a handicap there last time; still unexposed as a sprinter and he's a player if he can transfer his progress back to turf.
runners.comment: Record of 3212 since wind/gelding operations and he's strongly respected back on turf

runners.name: Big Time Rascal
runners.spotlight: Just one win from 22 starts but that came at Brighton (6f, good) last May and he was runner-up on AW at Lingfield last month; remains well handicapped on old form and he's in the mix back on turf.
runners.comment: Won at Brighton last spring and was runner-up on AW last month; in the mix

runners.name: Port Hedland
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on his win at Brighton (5.3f, good to firm) last May but he's 0-12 since and has finished down the field in most of those runs; opposable.
runners.comment: Won on turf last May but he's finished down the field in most of his runs since; opposable

runners.name: Vault Of Heaven
runners.spotlight: 14-race maiden but he returned to form with a creditable third at Wolverhampton (5f, AW) 17 days ago; that was more like it with blinkers added and has claims if he can back that up.
runners.comment: Back to form when third in blinkers at Wolverhampton and has claims if he can back that up

runners.name: Haworth Star
runners.spotlight: Looks interesting on his second at Newcastle (6f, Tapeta) in November but he took backward steps on final two runs last year; overall record is now 1-19 and he's yet to be placed in seven runs on turf; has work to do on return.
runners.comment: Has patchy profile and his record is 1-19; comes with risks attached on return

runners.name: Southbank
runners.spotlight: Won this race (firm) last year and he scored off 1lb lower at Kempton in the autumn; has remained in good form on AW this year and he chased home an improver at Lingfield (6f) last month; should get a good pace back on turf and he's a big player back at this track.
runners.comment: Won this last year and she's been in good form on AW this year; big player back on turf

runners.name: Washington Heir
runners.spotlight: Well held at 28-1 or bigger in all five runs including two handicaps at Wolverhampton (5f, Tapeta) this spring; now switches to turf and he needs a transformation back up in trip; hood worn last time is removed.
runners.comment: Well held at 28-1 or bigger in all five runs including two Tapeta handicaps this spring

runners.name: Little Miss Magic
runners.spotlight: Seemed to respond well to first-time visor when third at Chelmsford (6f) in January but she's not come close to that form in three runs since; her blindfold was removed late at Lingfield last month but she's now 1-27 and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Record of 1-27 and she's been well held in her last three runs; others preferred

runners.name: Tomarlo
runners.spotlight: Sole win was at Dundalk (6f, AW) in November 2024 but he made an encouraging start for new yard when fourth at Wolverhampton (5f, AW) 17 days ago; that was eye-catching from off the pace and has claims if he can build on that back on turf.
runners.comment: Eye-catching fourth on stable debut at Wolverhampton; has claims if he can build on that

runners.name: Coast
runners.spotlight: Winless since 2024 and she has failed to beat a rival in last three starts including over 5f here (firm) last week; not hard to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Winless since 2024 and has failed to beat a rival in her last three starts; no appeal

runners.name: Spirited Dancer
runners.spotlight: Won a C&D maiden (good) as a 2yo but her handicap career has not yet amounted to much and she was well held in three runs during the winter; mark continues to fall but she's now 1-13 and has plenty to prove after a break; cheekpieces removed.
runners.comment: Turf winner as 2yo but her handicap career has not yet amounted to much; down the list

runners.name: Comedian Leader
runners.spotlight: Better known as a 7f/1m performer but her latest win (last August) was gained over 6f; predictably found 5f too sharp at Lingfield (5f, AW) in March but finished well and wasn't beaten far; back up in trip today and has possibilities if she gets a good pace and some luck.
runners.comment: On dangerous mark and has possibilities if she gets a good pace and some luck

runners.name: Secret Handsheikh
runners.spotlight: Recorded his 11th win when scoring over C&D (firm) last August; well handicapped on that form but he's been up and down this year and was out the back at Windsor two weeks ago; comes with risks attached and he needs to bounce back again.
runners.comment: 11-time winner who is on a dangerous mark but he's hard to predict; others preferred

races.race_name: Blackmore Building Contractors Handicap (Bath Summer Sprint Series Qualifier) (Div II)

runners.name: Newyorkstateofmind
runners.spotlight: Nine-time winner who has not scored since September 2024 but he finished well to go close at Catterick (5f, good) last week; looks interesting off same mark and this step back up in trip is a positive.
runners.comment: Well handicapped on old form and runner-up at Catterick last week; highly respected

runners.name: Scent Of May
runners.spotlight: Turned over a 1-12 shot in Chepstow maiden (5f, good) last August but she's finished down the field in two handicaps since; still lightly raced and wasn't beaten far on her Nottingham reappearance last month but others are more convincing.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo but she's been well held in both handicaps and others are preferred

runners.name: Mister Sandman
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden who can take a strong hold but he finished well when runner-up over C&D (heavy) last autumn; returns after five months but he's handicapped on his best form and has had only three runs for current yard; needs a close look with hood added.
runners.comment: 0-10 but he's well treated on his second over C&D in October; needs close look on return

runners.name: Phoenix Beach
runners.spotlight: Tapeta specialist but he's hung right before finishing a well-held fifth at Wolverhampton (5f) in his last two starts; continues to edge down the weights but he's 0-13 on turf and has not run in this sphere since last May; others preferred.
runners.comment: Tapeta specialist but he's been well held on AW in last two starts and is 0-13 on turf

runners.name: Rinky Tinky Tinky
runners.spotlight: Won stable debut at Nottingham last April (6f, good to firm) but she's gone the wrong way since and was a well-held eighth in her final two runs last year; has something to prove but she's on a reduced mark and her win came after a similar-length absence; first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Just 1-14 but she's on a reduced mark and her win came after an absence; new headgear

runners.name: She Went Whoosh
runners.spotlight: Just one win from her 32 starts and recent efforts on AW and turf don't suggest she is primed to double her tally; down the list.
runners.comment: Record of 1-32 and recent efforts don't suggest she is primed to double her tally

runners.name: Bluesy Moon
runners.spotlight: 17-race maiden who has had plenty of chances off basement marks and was beaten 12l on her reappearance at Lingfield (6f, AW) last month; makes no appeal.
runners.comment: 17-race maiden who has struggled in her last four runs; makes no appeal

runners.name: He's An Angel
runners.spotlight: Hold-up performer who scored at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) in January but he's not hit the same level since and has finished eighth in his three runs (Tapeta/turf); on dangerous mark but he has something to prove and slow starts can be an issue.
runners.comment: On dangerous mark but he has something to prove and slow starts can be an issue

runners.name: Up The Anti
runners.spotlight: Versatile sort who won three times last year including at Leicester (1m, good to firm) and over C&D (heavy); now 2lb lower than for her AW win in December but things have not gone well in two runs this season; got hampered by a faller when pulled up at Lingfield on Tuesday but she needs a major revival back at this track; yard also runs Neptune Legend.
runners.comment: C&D winner but things have not gone well in her two runs this season; bit to prove

runners.name: Coco Hill
runners.spotlight: Winless since her 2yo days and she's finished down the field at Wolverhampton (5f, AW) in her three runs this year; losing run is now up 30 and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Winless since her 2yo days and has finished down the field in three runs on AW this year

runners.name: Sioux Warrior
runners.spotlight: Just one win from 24 starts but that was off 9lb higher at Brighton (5.3f, good) last spring; losing run is up to 13 but he's run some solid races on AW this year including when runner-up at Wolverhampton (7f) in March; seems tactically versatile and he's an interesting contender back in trip after a short break.
runners.comment: Well treated on his old form and was runner-up at Wolverhampton latest; key player

runners.name: Neptune Legend
runners.spotlight: Hasn't threatened in two AW runs for new yard this spring but he's now 6lb lower than for his last win in November and two of his three turf wins have come over C&D (good to firm); should get a good tow into this race and he's in the mix back at this track.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner and he's on a dangerous mark back at this track; in the mix

runners.name: Tilsworth Ony Ta
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner but his last success was in July 2024 and he has form figures of 87909 this year; opposable.
runners.comment: His last success was in July 2024 and has form figures of 87909 this year; opposable

runners.name: Titus Thor
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced 6yo but he's struggled at big prices in all of his runs including on handicap debut at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) in November; now switches to turf but he needs a transformation after six months off.
runners.comment: 6yo who has struggled at big prices in all four runs; lots to prove on turf/seasonal debut
