races.race_name: Download The Fairplay App Now Handicap

runners.name: Eutropia
runners.spotlight: No win in her first 13 starts but she's won three of her last four including at Bath (1m/1m2f) in her last two runs; up 3lb for her narrow success last time but she's open to more progress and is versatile ground-wise; big player back down in trip.
runners.comment: Improver who has won at Bath in her last two starts; big player back down in trip

runners.name: Rosemary's Rose
runners.spotlight: Won off 4lb lower at Kempton (1m, Polytrack) in March but she's been well held at Nottingham and Lingfield in her last two runs; has bit to prove again and she's now 0-8 on turf.
runners.comment: Won at Kempton in March but she's been well held last twice and others are preferred

runners.name: Oasis Sunrise
runners.spotlight: Just two wins from 30 starts but her last success was by 4l over C&D (firm) last month and she backed that up with a close third at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm); on same mark as last time and she should be in the thick of things again.
runners.comment: Won by 4l over C&D last month and was a close third over 1m2f last time; respected

runners.name: Antiquity
runners.spotlight: Ended a lengthy losing run when scoring at Lingfield (1m, Polytrack) in February; wasn't far off that level in next three runs but his form slumped at Lingfield three weeks ago; only 1lb higher than for last success but he needs to bounce back on this return to turf.
runners.comment: His form slumped on AW latest and he needs to bounce back near best
runners.owner: P&h Moorhead, J Collins And S Piper

runners.name: Super Hit
runners.spotlight: His last win was 13 months ago but he's run two promising races for James Owen this spring and was beaten less than 2l when sixth at Leicester (1m, good to firm; 9-2) last time; remains well handicapped on his old form and he shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: His last win was 13 months ago but he's run two promising races for James Owen this spring

runners.name: Tilani
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 4yo who got back on track when landing an apprentice handicap at Newbury (1m, good) last week and followed up in similar conditions at Yarmouth on Wednesday; has a penalty this time but he's on a roll and would be strongly respected if turned out quickly again.
runners.comment: Won at Newbury and Yarmouth in the last seven days; strongly respected under a penalty

runners.name: Rating
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-26 but her win was at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW) in December and she's finished placed at this track (1m/1m2f) in last two runs; did well to hang in there after getting pestered for the lead last time and she should go well again back in trip.
runners.comment: Won on AW in December and she's been placed at Bath in last two starts; shortlisted

runners.name: Havana's Dream
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in all 11 starts and she's been tailed in both runs this year including over C&D last month; first-time visor replaces cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Unplaced in all 11 starts and she's been tailed in both runs this year including over C&D

runners.name: Berning Hot
runners.spotlight: Overall record of 1-17 but she gave it a good shot under a prominent ride when runner-up at Kempton (1m, AW) two weeks ago; only 1lb higher back on turf and she could make another bold bid.
runners.comment: Record of 1-17 but she was a close second at Kempton last time; dangerous back on turf

runners.name: Amathus
runners.spotlight: All of his seven wins have been over shorter trips but he produced a strong finish when a 2l second over C&D (good) 18 days ago; on unchanged mark and has possibilities if he can find some extra spark.
runners.comment: All seven wins have been over shorter trips but was runner-up over C&D latest; dangerous

runners.name: Danehill Star
runners.spotlight: Improved his AW strike-rate to 6-29 when justifying favouritism at Wolverhampton (9.4f) in March; record of 0-8 on turf but he ran some good races on fast ground last summer and he's in the mix off only 2lb higher than last time.
runners.comment: Won at Wolverhampton in March and has claims if he can transfer that form back to turf

runners.name: Dappled Light
runners.spotlight: Hard to predict and was out the back on his C&D reappearance last month, but he may have needed that run and he won this race (firm) off 3lb higher last year; should get a good pace to aim at and he needs a close look in the market.
runners.comment: Hard to predict but he won this race off 3lb higher last year; one to keep an eye on

runners.name: Hammal
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability on his Wolverhampton debut in December 2024 but he's struggled at massive prices in two AW runs (7f/6f) this winter; this unexposed 5yo now goes handicapping but he needs a transformation at this new trip.
runners.comment: Unexposed 5yo but he needs a transformation upped to 1m on handicap debut

races.race_name: Randox Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Brohatna
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in Down Royal maiden hurdle on debut early last year; fair fifth of seven when set a stiff task behind Roc Dino here (2m6f) on return from an absence two weeks ago; could improve.
runners.comment: Set a stiff task behind Roc Dino here (2m6f) on return from an absence two weeks ago

runners.name: Le Questionnaire
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; placed in both point starts in Britain; beaten a long way on hurdles/stable debut at Fairyhouse; no match for easy winner at Tramore (16.5f, good to yielding) but finished clear of the remainder; high on the shortlist.
runners.comment: No match for winner at Tramore (16.5f, good to yielding) but finished clear of remainder

runners.name: Mr Master Mac
runners.spotlight: Promise on bumper debut at Roscommon before winning Sligo bumper (18.5f, good to yielding); moderate third of 10 at Tramore on hurdles bow on return (raced keenly); should improve.
runners.comment: Moderate third of 10 at Tramore on hurdles bow on return (raced keenly); should improve

runners.name: Holy Joe
runners.spotlight: Sent off the yard's second string for 2m4f Perth debut (wore hood to post) last month when held up in rear and always behind but no surprise to see major improvement given his connections; market will guide.
runners.comment: Didn't show much on Perth debut but no surprise if he found big improvement given stable

runners.name: Magical Sal
runners.spotlight: First reserve; well beaten in two bumpers and a point; much better when 7.75l fifth in course bumper two weeks ago; hurdles bow.
runners.comment: Much better when 7.75l fifth in course bumper two weeks ago; hurdles bow; reserve

runners.name: Cailin Deas
runners.spotlight: Finally rewarded on tenth bumper start with Kilbeggan win in August on good to yielding; saddle slipped when midfield on hurdles debut; unseated rider at the first at Thurles in November; first run for new yard after 183 days off.
runners.comment: Won 10th bumper start at Kilbeggan; excuses for two hurdles efforts to date; new yard

runners.name: Colwyn Bay
runners.spotlight: Outran 66-1 odds when third on debut in Clonmel bumper (2m3f, soft); took a backward step next time at Punchestown for all it was a much tougher assignment; hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Outran 66-1 odds when third on debut in Clonmel bumper but poor run at Punchestown

runners.name: Idaho Expresso
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-9 in points; pulled up on latest start in that sphere; major improvement required on rules debut.
runners.comment: 1-9 in points; pulled up on latest start in that sphere; major improvement required

runners.name: Yolanda's Star
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam unraced half-sister to smart bumper and 2m4f-2m5f hurdle/chase winner Livelovelaugh, out of point winner; best watched.
runners.comment: Dam half-sister to smart bumper and 2m4f-2m5f hurdle/chase winner Livelovelaugh; watch

runners.name: Tolka Row
runners.spotlight: Ran to a similar level in defeat in three points during the spring; ran respectably on hurdling debut when eighth of 17 at Clonmel (19.5f) eight days ago, staying on well without threatening; each-way possibilities.
runners.comment: Ran respectably on hurdling debut when eighth of 17 at Clonmel (19.5f) eight days ago

runners.name: Undertheinfluence
runners.spotlight: Court Cave gelding; 3,000euros 3yo; fifth foal; dam unraced; only of interest if the market speaks in his favour.
runners.comment: 3,000euros 3yo; 5th foal; dam unraced; only of interest if the market speaks in his favour

runners.name: Royal Mal
runners.spotlight: Some promise on third point start when third at Oldtown in February; raced keenly when beaten 33l into fifth on Cork bumper debut; needs to pull out more on hurdles bow.
runners.comment: Raced keenly when beaten 33l into fifth on Cork bumper debut; needs to pull out more

runners.name: Butch Coolidge
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; nice pedigree and has shown small hints of ability in maidens; hood goes on now.
runners.comment: Nice pedigree and has shown small hints of ability in maidens; hood goes on now; reserve

runners.name: Dahab
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in three Flat maidens for Noel Kelly; beaten 20l at Ayr on hurdling bow; tailed off on stable debut in first-time tongue-tie (retained) at Sligo 10 days ago; others preferred.
runners.comment: Runner-up in three Flat maidens for Noel Kelly; tailed off at Sligo on stable debut

runners.name: Echinacea
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; fair maiden on the Flat, runner-up in four of her last six starts at Dundalk since November; strong in the betting over C&D on hurdles debut a fortnight ago (sent off 9-2) but always in rear; still early days and better can be expected.
runners.comment: Fair maiden on Flat; strong in the betting for C&D hurdles debut but never a factor

runners.name: Glendaars Secret
runners.spotlight: Sent off at odds of 200-1 on Down Royal debut this month and ran accordingly; needs more time.
runners.comment: Sent off at odds of 200-1 on Down Royal debut this month and ran accordingly; needs time

runners.name: Beacon Bypass
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in a point, bumper and sole hurdles start; that form won't be enough.
runners.comment: Well beaten in a point, bumper and sole hurdles start; that form won't be enough

runners.name: Raleagh Scope
runners.spotlight: Never a factor in four starts under rules last year; failed to get involved in five points since February; blinkers; difficult to fancy.
runners.comment: Never a factor in four starts under rules last year and no show in five points; blinkers

races.race_name: Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Tell Us This
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D last July and came back to form when a comfortable winner of a Tramore claiming hurdle last time; needs to do more back in handicap company but yard is maintaining its good form so respected.
runners.comment: C&D winner won a Tramore claiming hurdle last time; needs to do more today but respected

runners.name: The Dancen Kid
runners.spotlight: Best run over hurdles when third in a Leopardstown handicap in testing ground in March; however, has been well below that level twice since and has to bounce back.
runners.comment: Third in a Leopardstown handicap in testing ground in March; well below that twice since

runners.name: Seskin Flash
runners.spotlight: A hurdle winner over 2m6f here last year; he has turned into a better chaser since (also winning here), rated 120; soundly beaten in that discipline at Punchestown last time but has to be of interest back hurdling off 10lb lower.
runners.comment: Course winner is rated 120 over fences so of interest back hurdling off 10lb lower

runners.name: Microscope
runners.spotlight: Ran above his previous efforts when beaten 13l at 150-1 in a Kilbeggan maiden hurdle last time; needs to show that wasn't a fluke on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Beaten 13l in a Kilbeggan maiden hurdle last time; needs to show that wasn't a fluke

runners.name: Sea Of Doubt
runners.spotlight: Tends to run well at this venue and got off the mark under this rider in a C&D handicap last time; raised 7lb but should be competitive off that mark.
runners.comment: Won a C&D handicap last time; raised 7lb but should be competitive off that mark

runners.name: Annie Agnew
runners.spotlight: Juvenile hurdle winner in 2023 has run well in defeat at times since; well beaten on return at Perth last time so will have to do a lot better.
runners.comment: Well beaten on return at Perth last time so will have to do a lot better

runners.name: Arcland
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner got off the mark over hurdles over C&D last September; raised 6lb for this first hurdle runs since; not a bad seasonal bow on the Flat recently.
runners.comment: C&D winner on last hurdle start; up 6lb; not a bad seasonal bow on the Flat recently
runners.owner: Exors of the Late E Blaney

runners.name: Aboras Mahler
runners.spotlight: Showed her first worthwhile form when a 20l runner-up in a C&D maiden hurdle last time; will have to build on that now handicapping.
runners.comment: 20l runner-up in a C&D maiden h'dle last time; will have to build on that now handicapping

runners.name: Centaq
runners.spotlight: Maiden has run well enough in defeat lately at Kilbeggan and Sligo (first-time cheekpieces) but has to find more dropped 1lb.
runners.comment: Has run well enough in defeat lately at Kilbeggan and Sligo; has to find more dropped 1lb

runners.name: I'll Be Handy
runners.spotlight: Modest Flat maiden but has run some fine races in defeat in juvenile hurdles against some smart rivals; not as good in a Grade 3 last time with cheekpieces on; this is easier so is one to consider.
runners.comment: Has run some fine races in defeat in juvenile hurdles against smart rivals; considered

runners.name: Bruant
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark over hurdles when winning a C&D novice hurdle last June; well below that level in two hurdle starts after; not a bad Navan Flat run recently; due to run in the 7.50 at Gowran Wednesday.
runners.comment: C&D winner last June was below form in two hurdle runs since; due to run Gowran Wednesday

races.race_name: Frank And Brian Fitzsimons Memorial Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Joya Del Mar
runners.spotlight: A 19-race maiden over hurdles but a player judged on C&D third off 13lb higher in March 2025 and good fourth at Down Royal on return off 8lb higher; sent off 13-2 when well beaten behind Gangster Granny latest but she's becoming well treated now.
runners.comment: Placed over C&D off 13lb higher last year; 13-2 when tailed off behind Gangster Granny lto
runners.owner: Mrs T Giles-Fahey & Miss S Mangan

runners.name: Yoursimplythebest
runners.spotlight: Showed next to nothing in maidens; placed on her first three handicaps before pulled up at Fairyhouse; found less than what looked likely when seventh of 16 at Tramore (2m5f) last month.
runners.comment: Found less than what looked likely when seventh of 16 at Tramore (2m5f) last month

runners.name: Toor Moon
runners.spotlight: Longstanding maiden has plenty of placed efforts to his name over fences and hurdles, including a C&D third in March from 2lb higher; below best in two starts since; place shout but hard to fancy for win purposes.
runners.comment: C&D third in March from 2lb higher; place shout but hard to fancy for win purposes

runners.name: Artic Chief
runners.spotlight: Had shown nothing prior to excellent third behind Gangster Granny over C&D on latest, and possibly might have won but hung badly left entering the straight (beaten 1.25l); front-runner has a place chance again from 2lb higher.
runners.comment: Third to Gangster Granny lto, hung badly up the straight; player from 2lb higher

runners.name: Gortmore Lady
runners.spotlight: Now 0-16 but hit the frame over C&D twice for Tony Martin off higher marks; pulled up on chase debut at Tramore when last seen in November; betting will guide on yard debut; tongue-tied.
runners.comment: Now 0-16 but hit the frame over C&D twice for Tony Martin off higher marks; new yard

runners.name: Bayonetta
runners.spotlight: Exposed mare; has won one of her 15 points but yet to win in 12 starts over hurdles, although has been placed on six occasions; only gave way late on over 26.5f at Perth nine days ago; should give her running again off lower Irish mark.
runners.comment: Only gave way late on over 26.5f at Perth nine days ago; should give her running

runners.name: Phildante
runners.spotlight: Maiden chased home a clear winner over 3m at Wexford last summer; rounded off last year with okay sixth of 16 at Tramore (2m5f) in November; off the track 178 days.
runners.comment: Maiden chased home a clear winner over 3m at Wexford last summer; back from a break

runners.name: Cozone
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; bounced back with a bang when easily winning over C&D off career-low mark on return in March; failed to back that up from 10lb higher at Down Royal.
runners.comment: Bolted up off career-low mark on return over C&D; sixth from 10lb higher lto; reserve

runners.name: Jimli's Cave
runners.spotlight: First reserve; sole win was over C&D in October 2024 off 86; hasn't shown a lot in recent outings.
runners.comment: Sole win over C&D in October 2024 off 86; hasn't shown a lot in recent outings; reserve

runners.name: Annarubyrose
runners.spotlight: Placed in a Sligo maiden for Gavin Cromwell; highly promising stable debut when runner-up to Gangster Granny over C&D on latest, headed close home; effectively 7lb better off with the winner factoring in the rider's claim so should go well.
runners.comment: Effectively 7lb better off with Gangster Granny for C&D second latest; big player

runners.name: Gangster Granny
runners.spotlight: Two Flat wins; gained first hurdles win when springing 33-1 surprise over C&D (good) a fortnight ago (prominent throughout); entitled to go well from 5lb higher mark; shortlisted.
runners.comment: 33-1 surprise win over C&D (good) a fortnight ago; entitled to go well from 5lb higher

runners.name: Milesfromthehonk
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; weak form in maidens; pulled up at Cork on handicap debut; never sighted at Fairyhouse in December; needs a transformation but early days for top stable.
runners.comment: Very weak form overall; needs a transformation but early days for top stable

runners.name: Lady Iseult
runners.spotlight: Sole win came over 2m5f at Roscommon in 2022; back from mammoth absence when pulled up at Down Royal last month; well beaten behind Gangster Granny over C&D on latest.
runners.comment: Sole win came over 2m5f at Roscommon in 2022; well beaten behind Gangster Granny lto

runners.name: Mercurial Sea
runners.spotlight: Fair form in bumpers early last season; best effort over hurdles yet when runner-up of 12 in course handicap (2m2f, good) two weeks ago, making most of the running; stamina not certain to hold out over this trip.
runners.comment: Runner-up in course handicap (2m2f, good) latest, making most of the running; stamina Qs

runners.name: Boston Queen
runners.spotlight: Beaten a cumulative distance of 131l in trio of maidens at 2m-2m2f on good and yielding ground; jumping issues evident when tailed off on handicap debut at Killarney; top connections and may have benefitted from the break.
runners.comment: No show in maidens or a handicap; top connections and may have benefitted from the break

runners.name: Scary Askari
runners.spotlight: Has shown ability including when runner-up in a course bumper; made a respiratory noise in running when soundly beaten on handicap debut at Fairyhouse; never involved at Punchestown in December.
runners.comment: Has shown ability including when runner-up in a course bumper; poor in handicaps so far

runners.name: Jetstream Jeff
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; yet to get closer than 49l behind the winner in four maiden runs; seems safe to rule out on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Yet to get closer than 49l behind the winner in 4 maiden runs; safe to rule out; reserve

runners.name: Game And Glory
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdles winner but was pulled up on her last visit to the track in a chase and pulled up last time over hurdles at Thurles.
runners.comment: Pulled up on her last visit to the track in a chase and pulled up last time over hurdles

races.race_name: Down Royal Corporation Of Horsebreeders Beginners Chase

runners.name: King Gris
runners.spotlight: Improved form after being switched to this yard, winning handicaps last December at Ayr and Hexham at around 2m4f on heavy; two moderate runs since including a fifth here; rated 92 over timber so has a bit to find on chase debut but hails from top yard.
runners.comment: Dual hurdles winner; rated 92 over timber so has a bit to find on chase debut but top yard

runners.name: Grace Faraday
runners.spotlight: Stays well; gave a stout hurdles performance when scoring over 3m at Galway in October; beaten in excess of 20l in her most recent three races; 90-rated hurdler may struggle over this trip on chase debut.
runners.comment: Stays well; 90-rated hurdler may struggle over this trip on chase debut

runners.name: Jerisk Star
runners.spotlight: Winner of Gowran handicap hurdle in October; rated 100 over hurdles; held when unseated at the last on chase debut in November; gave way before two out in Tramore beginners' next time; front-runner goes well fresh and could prove difficult to reel in on return.
runners.comment: Gowran hurdles win in October; last chase run full of promise; goes well fresh; big player

runners.name: Whispering Willow
runners.spotlight: Left all known form behind when bolting up over hurdles at Punchestown (2m, good to yielding) on stable/handicap debut nine days ago; one place and three non-completions in points but high on the shortlist on chase bow.
runners.comment: Bolted up on stable/handicap hurdle debut nine days ago; high on the shortlist; chase bow

runners.name: Giant Haystacks
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; top-rated of these over fences; third on sole point start; 1-13 over hurdles; tailed off on chase bow at Fairyhouse; improved fifth of 16 in Thurles handicap chase (2m2f, soft) in February; should be involved if the ground doesn't deteriorate.
runners.comment: Top-rated of these over fences; should be involved if the ground doesn't deteriorate

runners.name: Double Power
runners.spotlight: Record of 0-9 in points; best of four maiden hurdle runs was on debut at Leopardstown when sixth behind Frankie John; gained a mark of 100 over hurdles which gives him a squeak in this.
runners.comment: Best of four maiden hurdle runs was on debut; hurdles mark of 100 gives him a chance

runners.name: Auntie Lacey
runners.spotlight: Point winner for Colin Bowe; maiden over hurdles; encouragement taken from chasing debut when midfield at Thurles in October (18.5f, good to yielding); dam half-sister to dual Grade 1-winning chaser Conflated; tongue-tied on return.
runners.comment: Fair chase debut and bred to be a decent chaser; tongue-tied on return from break

runners.name: Coolboy Christy
runners.spotlight: Rated 99 over hurdles; promising fourth in Leopardstown novice handicap hurdle (2m3f, soft to heavy) in March before lesser effort at this venue; respectable fourth of 15 at Cork (2m3f, good) 12 days ago; 0-2 in points but bred to be a chaser; player.
runners.comment: 99-rated hurdler has run some good races in defeat; bred for chasing; player

runners.name: Pourquoi Poi
runners.spotlight: Won Punchestown handicap hurdle last August (2m4f, good to yielding) off 87; went backwards late on at Kilbeggan last time; dam out of half-sister to Irish Grand National winner Maid Of Money so an interesting chasing debutant.
runners.comment: Dam out of half-sister to Irish Grand National winner so an interesting chasing debutant

runners.name: Saunton Surf
runners.spotlight: Some decent form when trained in Britain; couple of fair efforts in handicap hurdles since joining this yard, including when fifth of 12 here (2m6f) last time; 99-rated hurdler should be in the mix if she takes to larger obstacles.
runners.comment: Some decent form overall; 99-rated hurdler should be in the mix if she takes to fences

runners.name: Jampot Lane
runners.spotlight: Hinted at some ability in three maiden hurdles; no time wasted in sending her chasing but doesn't look like an obvious winner.
runners.comment: No time wasted in sending her chasing now but doesn't look like an obvious winner

runners.name: Driveonwill
runners.spotlight: Now a 12-race maiden; ran a few decent races when in the frame in handicaps this year; unseated at the fourth in C&D handicap two weeks ago; might find a couple too good in this company.
runners.comment: Unseated at the fourth in C&D handicap two weeks ago; might find a couple too good here

runners.name: Massabielle
runners.spotlight: Maiden from 10 starts; modest 80-rated hurdler looks set to struggle on her chase bow.
runners.comment: Maiden from 10 starts; modest 80-rated hurdler looks set to struggle on her chase bow

runners.name: Art Of Silence
runners.spotlight: Ballinrobe maiden hurdle winner in 2023; returned from absence last year with two moderate Flat runs; tailed off over hurdles at Gowran; pulled up at Fairyhouse; not easy to fancy on chase debut.
runners.comment: Ballinrobe maiden hurdle winner in 2023; poor last year; chase bow; not easy to fancy

races.race_name: Down Royal Corporation Of Horsebreeders Maiden Hunters Chase

runners.name: West Of Idaho
runners.spotlight: Highly progressive pointer, winning four of his last five starts and won his last two by a combined 54l; his only defeat this year was arguably his best run when runner-up to Cause For Comment (several of these behind); will take a lot of beating here.
runners.comment: Highly progressive pointer, winning 4 of his last 5 and won his last two by a combined 54l

runners.name: Everest The Brave
runners.spotlight: Was coming there to win when falling at the last in Cragmore point in January but made no mistake on first run for Gavin Cromwell when scoring by 1l at Necarne 13 days ago; should give West Of Idaho most to think about.
runners.comment: Unlucky on debut when faller at the last; won next time at Necarne; can go well

runners.name: Weespoof
runners.spotlight: Solid pointer who is 3-13 with six further placings; 28l behind West Of Idaho when runner-up at Necarne a fortnight ago; cheekpieces are tried now.
runners.comment: 28l behind West Of Idaho when runner-up at Necarne a fortnight ago; cheekpieces are tried

runners.name: Mister Frostie
runners.spotlight: Hints of ability in maiden hurdles for this stable; won a maiden point at Hexham last November; hard to fancy on the form of his run since returning to Colin McBratney.
runners.comment: Hard to fancy on the form of his run since returning to Colin McBratney

runners.name: Sledgehammer
runners.spotlight: Narrow winner of a maiden point at Tyrella in February after two failures to complete; tailed off on hunter chase debut at Down Royal; pulled up over C&D next time after bad mistake; distant sixth at Tramore last month.
runners.comment: Tall order here and was pulled up over C&D on his penultimate run; others preferred

runners.name: Vodka Splash
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 12 point starts at Ballindenisk in January; well behind Sledgehammer in Tramore hunters' chase; holds very little chance here; tongue-tied.
runners.comment: Well behind Sledgehammer in Tramore hunters'; holds very little chance here; tongue-tied

runners.name: Cebu City
runners.spotlight: A win, four runner-ups and a third to his name from eight point runs, the victory on latest at Stowlin when keeping on well to score by 5l; entitled to respect off the back of that but still has plenty to find to trouble West Of Idaho.
runners.comment: Finally got first point win from eight tries at Stowlin on latest; plenty to find though

races.race_name: Albert Bartlett Hunters Chase

runners.name: Cause For Comment
runners.spotlight: Point winner was just denied by stablemate Asphalt Cowboy in this race last year when favourite; won a maiden hunter chase here over 3m in March but couldn't cope with the rise in class at Punchestown since; this is easier and stable selected.
runners.comment: Was 2nd in this last year; outclassed at Punchestown last time; this is easier; a player

runners.name: Asphalt Cowboy
runners.spotlight: Two point wins plus two hunter chase wins, notably this race last year; however, has been poor on his two recent starts and looks to be the yard's second string.
runners.comment: Won this last year but poor on his two recent starts; looks to be the yard's second string

runners.name: Gorthill
runners.spotlight: Four point wins and won a 3m hunter chase here in 2023; however, can't be fancied on his point form of last season.
runners.comment: Won a hunter chase here in 2023 but can't be fancied on his point form of last season

runners.name: Montecam
runners.spotlight: Useful hurdler up to last season ran his best race in three points when third in an open race at Toomebridge last time; ran well enough on sole chase start in 2024 but has to do more.
runners.comment: Third in an open race last time; ran well enough on sole chase start in 2024; needs more

runners.name: Our Story
runners.spotlight: A record of only 2-16 in points and has been well held on both hunter chase outings this year; cheekpieces need to bring improvement.
runners.comment: Well held on both hunter chase outings this year; cheekpieces need to bring improvement

runners.name: Fortune Lad
runners.spotlight: Won for the third time in points at Loughanmore last time, easily, albeit against only two rivals; still respected on rules debut for a yard that does well in these races and looks their first string.
runners.comment: Easily won third point last time; respected on rules debut for a yard that does well here

runners.name: Dorking Cock
runners.spotlight: Has won three over hurdles and fences; ran well at times in defeat in points and hunter chases last season but has to do much better today.
runners.comment: Ran well at times in defeat in points and hunter chases last season; has to do much better

runners.name: Ramillies
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; six-time point winner has been well below his best in that sphere last season and isn't easy to fancy on that form.
runners.comment: Well below his best in points last season and isn't easy to fancy on that form

races.race_name: Caherty Stables Racing Syndicate Point-To-Point INH Flat Race

runners.name: Majestic Horizon
runners.spotlight: Won his sole point start at Rathcannon last winter for Cormac Doyle and the form has been franked on the track since; finished 2.5l in front of today's rival Brootally when third in a Wexford bumper recently; can improve from that and be a major player.
runners.comment: Point winner was 3rd in a Wexford bumper; can improve from that and be a major player

runners.name: Dinner Dance
runners.spotlight: Won her sole point start at Aghabullogue in January on heavy ground for Patrick Kiely; makes rules debut for this top yard on different ground but should cope as a half-sister to Group 2 Flat scorer Safe Voyage; big player.
runners.comment: Point winner is a half-sister to a Group 2 Flat winner; looks a big player on bumper bow

runners.name: Theemeraldempress
runners.spotlight: Just denied on point debut for Sean Doyle at Loughanmore after making most of the running; sold for £70,000 since and makes bumper debut for a top yard; comes from the family of Faugheen; respected.
runners.comment: Just denied on point debut; sold for £70,000 since; from the family of Faugheen; player
runners.owner: Ms D M McGuire/Oak Lodge Farm Ltd/G King/Garrett Dillon

runners.name: Bearys Cross
runners.spotlight: Form figures in points aren't inspiring but was still in the lead at the second-last when unseating at Ballysteen last time; could run better than expectations.
runners.comment: Led at the second-last when unseating in a point last time; could run above expectations

runners.name: Im Too Busy
runners.spotlight: Pulled up then beaten a long way in two point runs so isn't easy to fancy on bumper debut.
runners.comment: Pulled up then beaten a long way in two point runs so isn't easy to fancy on bumper debut

runners.name: Juarno
runners.spotlight: The best of two point runs when beaten 8l at Necarne last time; there is a bumper winner in his pedigree but needs to find lots of improvement.
runners.comment: Beaten 8l in a point last time and needs to find lots of improvement

runners.name: Brootally
runners.spotlight: Won a point on fourth start last month on his first run in almost a year; solid bumper debut at Wexford early this month and should step forward from that so can be a player.
runners.comment: Point winner ran well on bumper debut recently; should step forward from that; a player

races.race_name: Book For Fathers Day Sunday 21st June Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Naval Light
runners.spotlight: He has been expensive to follow thus far but he has been consistent in terms of his RPR and this is easier than the Newmarket handicap he contested three weeks ago; cheekpieces experiment quickly aborted; well drawn and he holds strong claims.
runners.comment: Expensive to follow but he sets a good standard and holds strong claims in this field

runners.name: Stoneacre Joe
runners.spotlight: Looked a different proposition in his first two runs this year when second at Doncaster (100-1) and Ayr (higher RPR); looked open to further progress but soundly at Haydock (soft; his first run away from good to firm ground) on Thursday and drawn widest here.
runners.comment: Second at Doncaster and Ayr but well beaten on soft ground at Haydock yesterday

runners.name: Viper
runners.spotlight: Confirmed the promise of his Doncaster debut (7f, good to soft) when finishing a close third at Newcastle (7f) three weeks ago; drop in trip shouldn't be a problem and he holds each-way claims once again.
runners.comment: Two encouraging runs over 7f; any improvement for the drop in trip will see him involved

runners.name: Lineofsuccession
runners.spotlight: First foal of a maiden from the family of Sinndar; 6f looks on the sharp side given her pedigree.
runners.comment: Dam a maiden from the family of Sinndar; 6f likely to be an inadequate test on debut

runners.name: Lady Vega
runners.spotlight: 80,000euros yearling; sixth foal; half-sister to winners Flashcard (Group-placed 6f-1m including 2yo; RPR 110), Look Back Smiling (6f-1m including 2yo/AW; 94) and Action Motion (2m hurdle); dam unraced half-sister to useful 6f-1m winner Mucho Applause; she has a useful standard to aim at on debut but her pediigree offers hope.
runners.comment: 80,000euros half-sister to three winners; she has a useful standard to aim at on debut

runners.name: King's Road Dandy
runners.spotlight: Third of eight in a C&D maiden on sole 2yo start last August; absent until reappearing at Doncaster four weeks ago (gelded after debut), a race where he failed to get home having taken a strong hold; slower ground to deal with today; improvement essential.
runners.comment: Similar level of form in two fast-ground 6f runs 261 days apart; improvement essential

runners.name: Leelawadee
runners.spotlight: 33-1 for her Newcastle debut last month (7f) and dropped away having taken a keen hold; may need more time.
runners.comment: 33-1 when weakening into seventh on Newcastle debut last month (7f); may need more time

runners.name: Tommie's Angel
runners.spotlight: Ran to a modest level on her Southwell debut (6f) two weeks ago; longer-term prospect.
runners.comment: Ran to a modest level on her Southwell debut (6f) two weeks ago; longer-term prospect

runners.name: Star With Purpose
runners.spotlight: Promise in a couple of 6f runs this spring (good to soft and good to firm) but that form lags well short of what will be required today; blinkers now added.
runners.comment: Signs of ability in two 6f runs this spring; new blinkers need to have a big effect though

races.race_name: Juddmonte EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B-D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Too Many Spirits
runners.spotlight: Market mover on his Thirsk debut but he needed the experience and could only manage fifth; much better when second of eight here (5f, good) 31 days ago, keeping on well having run green when initially coming off the bridle; 6f should suit and he sets the standard.
runners.comment: Kept on well for second over 5f here last month; sets the standard and can do better at 6f

runners.name: Ricky Punting
runners.spotlight: Promise at Bath on debut and again when plugging on for second at Hamilton last week (6f, good); remains capable of better and he's high on the list.
runners.comment: Second at Hamilton last week (6f, good); open to further progress; high on the list

runners.name: Mr Minz
runners.spotlight: 70,000euros yearling; second foal; closely related to 6f 2yo winner French Affair (RPR 78); dam a modest four-race maiden (64); stable's 3% strike-rate with turf 2yos in the last five seasons tempers enthusiasm.
runners.comment: 70,000euros close relation of 6f 2yo winner French Affair (RPR 78); yard 3% with turf 2yos

runners.name: College Boy
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal of an unraced half-sister to useful US 1m-1m1f winner Maximova; stable's newcomers usually better for their debut.
runners.comment: Only minor appeal on paper and yard's newcomers tend to be better for a run

runners.name: Fasthope
runners.spotlight: 5,000euros foal, 6,000euros yearling; eleventh foal; half-brother to five winners including Tamadhor (7f including 2yo; RPR 104), Bawaasil (7f/1m; 89), Madroos (1m; 83) and Alnaas (7f AW; 81); dam 8.5f winner (86), half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 2 winner Memory; stable yet to hit the target with 2yos this year (0-8).
runners.comment: 6,000euros half-brother to five winners out of an 8.5f winner; yard 0-8 with 2yos in 2026

runners.name: Le Grand Etoile
runners.spotlight: 17,000euros foal, 52,000gns yearling; ninth foal; half-brother to six winners including Zannda (1m2f-1m4f including Group 3; RPR 103), Zanndabad (1m1f-10.5f Flat including 2yo, 101; useful 2m4f-2m6f hurdle) and Zenya (1m2f; 88); dam 1m4f AW winner (75); enough paper appeal to enter calculations.
runners.comment: 52,000gns half-brother to six winners (including Group 3); likely type on breeding

runners.name: Real Edition
runners.spotlight: 20,000gns yearling; eighth foal; half-brother to six winners including Real Force (5f-7.5f including 2yo; RPR 109), Real Gain (1m-1m2f including 2yo/AW; 107), Av A Word (1m2f; 75) and Hic Bibi (5.5f-7f; including 2yo/AW; 70); dam unraced half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 3 winner Camargo; likely type on paper but stable 0-8 (none placed) with 2yos this year.
runners.comment: 20,000gns half-brother to six winners; yard 0-8 (all unplaced) with 2yos this year

runners.name: Adoramus
runners.spotlight: 12-1 for his Lingfield debut three weeks ago (6f, AW), a race where he kept on stealthily to take third; open to plenty of improvement but it will be needed.
runners.comment: Good late headway to take third at Lingfield (6f, AW) three weeks ago; open to improvement
runners.owner: Mr And Mrs R Everist And Partner

runners.name: Green Sovereign
runners.spotlight: Promise amidst inexperience when fifth of nine on his Kempton debut (6f, AW) 18 days ago; well drawn and could take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Green when fifth of nine on his Kempton debut 18 days ago; could take a big step forward

runners.name: Arrbob
runners.spotlight: His two runs over 5f suggest this longer trip will suit; can do better but he will need a good deal more to win.
runners.comment: Unplaced in two 5f runs; should be suited by 6f but improvement is essential

races.race_name: FBS Chartered Accountants Handicap

runners.name: Jojo Rabbit
runners.spotlight: Made all to win a Class 6 handicap over C&D last month; not so good on his next two starts but returned to his recent best when a close second at Beverley ten days ago; runs off the same mark today and he's ideally drawn to attack; should go well.
runners.comment: Conditions no problem and he went close off this mark at Beverley ten days ago; well drawn
runners.owner: Grange Park Racing XIII & Ruth Carr

runners.name: Lucius Aurelius
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 17th attempt when leading late at Catterick last month (5f, good); beat a subsequent winner on that occasion but a 5lb rise might prove beyond him.
runners.comment: 17th time lucky when winning at Catterick last month; 5lb rise might find him out
runners.owner: John Kelly, John McNeill & Kevin Mowatt

runners.name: Ay Gee Ell
runners.spotlight: Blitzed his rivals at Southwell in December (5f) when a visor was first used; generally run well in defeat since but he will need to pull out more to take this prize.
runners.comment: Impressed in 1st-time visor (5f, AW) in December; many good runs since; needs more to win

runners.name: Betweenthesticks
runners.spotlight: He has returned from a six-month break in rude health, winning at Wolverhampton and over C&D (second C&D success) last month and running well in his hat-trick bid at Ripon a fortnight ago; wide draw not ideal for a front-runner but he isn't handicapped out of things; trainer also runs Jojo Rabbit.
runners.comment: Two wins, including C&D, and a second from three runs this year; respected despite draw
runners.owner: J A Swinburne & Mrs Ruth A Carr

runners.name: Mrs Trump
runners.spotlight: Last summer's two wins came over 6f, the second of them off this mark; should come on for her recent return to action but others appeal more for win purposes.
runners.comment: Two 6f wins last summer, the second of them off this mark; needs to build on recent return
runners.owner: R Chapman, G J Daly & R J Parks

runners.name: King's Crown
runners.spotlight: Two AW wins this winter; never in the hunt at Wetherby last month on his return to turf but he is effective over C&D and seems to handle any ground; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Not at his best back on turf last month but he has often run well at this track

runners.name: Knicks
runners.spotlight: Over two years since the second of his two wins; lurks on a dangerous mark though and his two runs this year have seen him finish within 3l of the winner; has his first run at Pontefract but he is effective around a left-hand turn; can't rule out from a handy draw.
runners.comment: On a losing run but on a dangerous mark and he can't be ruled out from a handy draw

runners.name: Fuji Mountain
runners.spotlight: Won twice at Chester as a 2yo; just missed out over C&D last month (blinkers first time) when finding only Betweenthesticks too strong; ran okay at Beverley next time and he was drawn wide when disappointing back there ten days ago; stable also run Changeofmind.
runners.comment: Missed out by a short-head over C&D last month; draw excuse latest; stable runs two here

runners.name: Ventura Express
runners.spotlight: Four of his six wins have come over C&D, including the corresponding race in 2024; drew a blank in 2025 but retains ability and he has run some solid races in defeat this year too, keeping on for fourth at Beverley 18 days ago; drops in class but he has the widest stall to contend with.
runners.comment: Four-time C&D winner, including this race in 2024; stall 10 dents confidence today

runners.name: Changeofmind
runners.spotlight: Won his first two starts but beaten 17 times since; down the field at Thirsk on last week's reappearance but drops in grade today and the slower ground should suit.
runners.comment: Beaten 17 times since winning first two starts; drops in class on ground that should suit

races.race_name: Thank You Alexia And Good Luck Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Trojan Soldier
runners.spotlight: Improved for a step up in trip and then cheekpieces, getting off the mark at Southwell (1m6f) last month and following up at Ripon on Sunday (2m, good); escapes a penalty for that win and today's further rise in distance could spark additional progress; possible he could do with the ground drying out a bit but he's on the up and should make a bold bid to complete his hat-trick.
runners.comment: 2-2 since cheekpieces went on; unpenalised for Sunday's Ripon win; drying ground will help

runners.name: Maghlaak
runners.spotlight: Made a winning start for this yard in a novice handicap chase at Southwell (2m4f, soft) last November; finished a long way behind Sea Of Kings at Doncaster on his return to the Flat four weeks ago; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Won a chase on stable debut in November; well held on last month's return to the Flat

runners.name: Wahraan
runners.spotlight: Quirky and sometimes blows the start; on a reduced mark and retains some ability but he is not the obvious solution to this puzzle.
runners.comment: Can blow the start; not the force of old and others have more pressing claims

runners.name: Diamand De Vindecy
runners.spotlight: Ran away with a 2m2f handicap here last April; not quite so good since, including when well held back on the Flat at Ripon last month.
runners.comment: 2m2f winner here last April; not so good since and others should have his measure today

runners.name: Sea Of Kings
runners.spotlight: 0-6 but he has recorded an RPR of 84 or more in each of his last five starts; beaten at odds-on at Doncaster on his reappearance (1m4f, good to firm) four weeks ago; stepping up to a marathon trip could prompt further progress, as could the new cheekpieces; shortlisted.
runners.comment: 0-6 but he has shown a good level of ability; new trip/headgear could prompt improvement

runners.name: Action Pact
runners.spotlight: Improved as his stamina was drawn out last season, winning over 2m2f here in October (soft); below par on extremely deep ground at Nottingham when last seen 211 days ago but that is not the run to judge him on; unlikely he has reached his ceiling and he's an interesting contender.
runners.comment: 2m2f winner here last October; unexposed and he's an interesting contender

runners.name: Vaguely Royal
runners.spotlight: Comes here on the back of two third-place finishes but the latest was behind Trojan Soldier and there is no obvious reason why the placings would be reversed.
runners.comment: 3rd on last two starts but he has 3.75l to find with Trojan Soldier on Sunday's Ripon run

races.race_name: WTJ Insurance Brokers 40th Anniversary Handicap

runners.name: Havachoc
runners.spotlight: Completed a 1m4f AW hat-trick in January/February; series of good runs in defeat since, the latest when second at Wolverhampton two weeks ago; cheekpieces now given a go.
runners.comment: AW hat-trick this winter and solid in defeat since; solid chance with headgear fitted

runners.name: Blondina
runners.spotlight: Modest maiden on the Flat in Ireland for Jim Bolger and showed little in three hurdles for current yard this year; last month's return to the Flat was much more encouraging though, keeping on strongly over 1m4f at Musselburgh, and she now has something to build on.
runners.comment: Good 3rd at Musselburgh on first Flat run for the yard; she now has something to build on

runners.name: Lillistar
runners.spotlight: Not at her best over hurdles or here (extended 2m5f) last month; drop in trip can help but others appeal more for win purposes.
runners.comment: Not at best Flat or hurdles last month; drop in trip can help but others appeal more

runners.name: Anificas Beauty
runners.spotlight: Five wins at up to 9.4f; has form at 1m3f but suspicion there will be stronger stayers in the field.
runners.comment: 5 wins at up to 9.4f; stays 1m3f but likely there will be stronger stayers in the line up

runners.name: Muhib
runners.spotlight: 0-22 but he arrives in good form, third in minor handicaps at Beverley and Catterick on his last two starts; more minor money could be forthcoming.
runners.comment: 22-race maiden but he arrives on the back of two solid 3rd-place finishes; each-way shout

runners.name: Apache Eagle
runners.spotlight: 0-17 and he blows hot and cold but there have been some good runs in defeat on AW this year; finished behind Havachoc at Wolverhampton two weeks ago and may do so again.
runners.comment: Exposed maiden; some good AW runs this year but behind Havachoc last time; opposable

runners.name: Hickton
runners.spotlight: Took a while to get off the mark but he has won three of his last seven starts (1m4f-1m6f); poor run over 2m at Southwell two weeks ago; capable of bouncing back now dropping in distance.
runners.comment: Three wins from his last seven runs but he ran poorly over 2m last time; can bounce back

runners.name: Dubai Venture
runners.spotlight: Made all to win the corresponding race 12 months ago off a 6lb lower mark; followed up at Beverley in July and only just denied in his hat-trick bid when last seen 309 days ago; races off the same mark here and there should be more to come this year.
runners.comment: Won this race last year & continued on the up afterwards; not yet exposed; absent 309 days

races.race_name: Summer Sutton 18th Birthday Celebration Handicap

runners.name: Arkenstaar
runners.spotlight: He was gaining his first win away from Hamilton when landing a C&D handicap last October off a 2lb lower mark; promising return to action at his beloved Hamilton 19 days ago; that should have blown away any cobwebs and he rates a strong contender for a yard who could be hitting top gear judged on recent results.
runners.comment: C&D win last October worked out well; good return at Hamilton; yard hitting top form

runners.name: Mount King
runners.spotlight: Three of his four wins have come over C&D, including in this race 12 months ago; 1lb lower than for his last win and he should be at concert pitch with two runs under his belt; one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Three C&D wins including this race last year; 1lb below last winning mark; contender

runners.name: Moonjid
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with a breakthrough win at Nottingham (8.3f, good); ran his best race since when fourth back at Nottingham five weeks ago (cheekpieces first time); needs more to take this but it wouldn't be out of the question.
runners.comment: Nottingham winner last October off 4lb lower; best run since when 4th in headgear latest

runners.name: Powerful Response
runners.spotlight: Back to winning ways with a front-running C&D success 23 days ago (visored first time); up 2lb but should give it another good go.
runners.comment: Made all to win over C&D last month (visored first time); 2lb rise manageable

runners.name: Keats House
runners.spotlight: Won two Class 6 handicaps at around 1m in the second half of 2025; creditable efforts off his revised mark to end that campaign and he should be sharper with last month's return to action behind him; others still appeal more for win purposes.
runners.comment: Two Class 6 wins last season; sharper for last month's return but others still appeal more

runners.name: No Knee Never
runners.spotlight: Four wins (6f to 8.6f) on AW since December; perhaps stretched by 1m2f when only fourth at Ripon a fortnight ago; returning to a mile should help but a career best is needed to defy this mark.
runners.comment: Four AW wins since December; return to 1m can help but no easy task off revised mark

runners.name: Callisto Dream
runners.spotlight: Won twice in novice company before running creditably in 1m handicaps at Ascot and Redcar last July; both subsequent runs have resulted in heavy defeats over 1m2f; returns from 222 days off at a lower level and back at a mile; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with two heavy defeats over 1m2f; down in trip/class for her return; chance

runners.name: Hopeful Joy
runners.spotlight: Looked rusty on her seasonal/handicap debut at Beverley ten days ago (8.4f, good to firm; 40-1); kept on well enough to think this mark was in range; not yet exposed.
runners.comment: No threat on recent seasonal/h'cap debut but she was keeping on at the finish; unexposed

runners.name: Selection
runners.spotlight: 6f course win as a 2yo; added a 1m handicap at Newcastle last October and there have been some near misses on AW this year too; not beaten far into fifth at Thirsk on Saturday on her return to Class 5 level; well drawn but slower than good would be a concern.
runners.comment: Course winner; not beaten far at Thirsk on Saturday; good draw; drying ground would help

runners.name: Distinction
runners.spotlight: Conditions no problem and he has slipped back down the weights but this year's efforts leave him with a point to prove.
runners.comment: Conditions fine and slipping back down the weights but yet to shine in 2026

races.race_name: CopyBet And Worcester Partners For 2026 Handicap Chase (ARC Summer Chase Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Zero Tolerance
runners.spotlight: 17-race maiden under rules; reached the frame on numerous occasions over fences but he was beaten 21l when third at Hexham (1m7f, good to soft) two weeks ago; should be fine back on the forecast quicker ground but he needs to get back near his best.
runners.comment: 17-race maiden under rules and was a well-held third at Hexham last time; others preferred

runners.name: Applicationofcash
runners.spotlight: 0-7 over hurdles but he made it fourth-time lucky over fences when scoring on his stable debut at Taunton (2m, good) in March; looked fortunate because the leader made a bad mistake at the last but he's only 2lb higher here and he had an excuse when stepped back up 2m4f last time; dangerous back in trip.
runners.comment: Won at Taunton and he had an excuse when stepped back up to 2m4f latest; respected

runners.name: Electric Eddy
runners.spotlight: Won a 2m handicap hurdle here (good) in September 2024; pulled up in two of his four starts since but he was runner-up over fences at Hereford (2m, good) on his penultimate run; that form puts him in the picture and this unexposed chaser is not ruled out after another break.
runners.comment: Hard to predict but was runner-up over fences (2m) on his penultimate run; in the mix

runners.name: Alba Crus
runners.spotlight: Good-ground bumper winner who was placed in a 2m3f novice hurdle last season; ran poorly on recent handicap hurdle debut (2m4f) and now has a point to prove but might be aided by first-time tongue-tie and connections are evidently keen to switch him to fences.
runners.comment: Unexposed 5yo and he needs a close look with tongue-tie added on chase debut

runners.name: Bongo Man
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden who has been inconsistent over hurdles but he was a close second in a Huntingdon handicap (2m, good; tongue-tie added) on his penultimate run; on same mark here and has possibilities if he can recapture that form on this switch to fences.
runners.comment: 0-9 but he's in the mix on best hurdles form; needs watching in market on chase debut

races.race_name: Safer Gambling At CopyBet Handicap Chase (ARC Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Ukantango
runners.spotlight: Triple hurdle winner and chase winner (2m4f), on good or slower; that chase win came readily on his penultimate start, at Southwell; unable to back that up after a 7lb rise and step up in grade at Uttoxeter (2m5f, good) latest; still well treated on his winning effort, if he can repeat it.
runners.comment: Ready winner at Southwell on his penultimate start; below par latest; still well treated.

runners.name: Lermoos Legend
runners.spotlight: Experienced chaser with a good strike rate (9-48 and placed on 20 other occasions); returned from a winter break not far off his best recent form when 1l second at Ffos Las (2m3f, good) last month; up 2lb but should again be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Not far off his best recent form latest; up 2lb but should again be thereabouts.

runners.name: Jet Smart
runners.spotlight: C&D winner (good) last summer when 11lb lower and successfully followed up at Uttoxeter (2m4f, good), when 5lb lower; after a successful stint in points, he returned to rules last month equalling his career best when second at Uttoxeter (2m5f, good); up 3lb but likely to remain competitive.
runners.comment: C&D winner who equalled his career best latest; up 3lb but likely to remain competitive.

runners.name: Fine Casting
runners.spotlight: Below par when returning to hurdling at Southwell (2m4f, good) in March; has dropped 2lb since finishing fourth at Wetherby (2m3f, soft) on his most recent chase start and has claims if able to reproduce the form of that or his Bangor success (2m4f, soft) last November; acts on a sound surface.
runners.comment: Claims if reproducing the form of his Wetherby fourth or his Bangor win last season.

runners.name: Karnaval Point
runners.spotlight: 2m7f and more recently 2m2f chase winner when trained in France, both on testing ground; has changed hands since last month's Auteuil success; sound surface an unknown; appears to have a workable mark on his first start for this trainer; market check.
runners.comment: 2m7f and more recently 2m2f chase winner in France; workable mark; market check advised.

runners.name: Atreides
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 22nd attempt in a Plumpton chase (2m4f, good to soft; first-time tongue-tie and visor) last December; avoided a penalty and successfully followed up at Fontwell (2m3f, heavy); matched that effort at Wincanton (2m4f, heavy) latest; acts on good; each-way player.
runners.comment: Dual slow-ground chase winner at around 2m4f; good effort latest; each-way player.

runners.name: Country Park
runners.spotlight: Point winner who is 0-13 under rules; has some useful hurdles form to his name, with his best effort coming on good ground and over a similar trip to this; on a handy mark for this chase debut if better for a recent hurdles start; check the market for clues.
runners.comment: Handy mark for this chase debut if better for a recent hurdles start; check the market.

runners.name: Melton Mossy
runners.spotlight: Multiple hurdle winner at up to 2m5f on a sound surface; 1-2 over fences; won a three-runner chase at Wincanton (1m7f, good) when last seen in March; encouraging record after a break; up 3lb and is unexposed as a chaser over this trip; player.
runners.comment: Won a three-runner Wincanton chase (1m7f, good) latest; up 3lb and unexposed; player.

runners.name: Disco Davis
runners.spotlight: Heavy-ground 2m hurdle winner when with Dan Skelton; changed hands for 1,000gns since being beaten more than 16l in a Stratford seller in March; 2lb lower for chase debut but can only suggest a watching brief at present.
runners.comment: 2m hurdle winner; changed hands for 1,000gns after selling hurdle defeat; watching brief.

races.race_name: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB)

runners.name: Closing Time
runners.spotlight: 26,000euros foal, 50,000euros 3yo; third foal; half-brother to point/2m hurdle winner Knock Off Soxs; dam placed twice 9.4f AW (RPR 72), half-sister to smart dual-purpose performer Blue Bajan (won 2m Group 2 on Flat and 2m Swinton Hurdle); trainer 19% in bumpers (five seasons); market check.
runners.comment: Half-brother to point/2m hurdle winner Knock Off Soxs; trainer 19% in bumpers.

runners.name: Faithful Guardian
runners.spotlight: 5-2 when second on debut in a Ludlow bumper (good) last November; below that level in a Listed event at Cheltenham (good) in January; has since left Alan King and now has his first start for a trainer who is 13% in bumpers over five seasons; market check.
runners.comment: Second on debut; below that level in Listed bumper latest; new trainer 13% in bumpers.

runners.name: Olly's Icon
runners.spotlight: 25-1 on debut when racing freely and finishing 4l second in a Warwick bumper (good) last month; that offered some encouragement that he can pick up a similar contest, if able to settle.
runners.comment: 25-1 on debut when racing freely and 4l second at Warwick; encouraging; needs to settle.

runners.name: Pether's Angel
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam unraced half-sister to French winners Saint Goustan Blue (smart and prolific 2m-2m6f hurdle/chase and 3m3f Grade 1 chase runner-up), Malineglaces (2m2f Listed hurdle), Saint Goustan (2m1f-2m5f hurdle/chase) and Vision Sainte (2m1f jump); stable 13% in bumpers (five seasons); possible player on debut.
runners.comment: Dam unraced half-sister to four French winners; stable 13% in bumpers (five seasons).

runners.name: Turbogeist
runners.spotlight: 22,000euros foal; ninth foal; closely related to winners Flying Fortune (useful bumper and 2m3f/2m4f hurdle) and Turbocharged (bumper); dam smart bumper and 1m4f Flat winner (including Group 2; RPR 113); trainer 21% in bumpers since taking out a licence; interesting.
runners.comment: Closely related to two bumper winners; trainer 21% in bumpers; interesting.

runners.name: Mossy Moon
runners.spotlight: 17,000euros foal, 50,000euros 3yo; first foal; dam well held in 2m4f/3m hurdles, half-sister to winners Lady Tadita (bumper/2m hurdle), Gitche Gumee (bumper/2m hurdle) and Mythical Moon (2m hurdle); trainer, who is 15% in bumpers (five seasons), has his string in good form.
runners.comment: 17,000euros foal, 50,000euros 3yo; dam well held but her half-sisters won bumpers/hurdles.
runners.owner: The Lee Family, A Stennett & J Wright

runners.name: C'Est Pour Moi
runners.spotlight: £14,000 3yo; third foal; dam runner-up 2m4f chase in France, half-sister to French winners Ciel Bleu Azur (2m2f-2m5f hurdle/chase), Coline Princesse (2m1f chase) and Captain Jack (2m3f/2m4f chase); hooded on debut; stable 15% in bumpers in last two seasons; market check.
runners.comment: £14,000 3yo; third foal; dam runner-up 2m4f chase; stable 15% in bumpers last two seasons.

runners.name: Mister Forb
runners.spotlight: Third foal; half-brother to 2m hurdle/3m chase winner The Grey Man; dam 2m5f hurdle/2m6f chase winner, sister to smart 2m-2m6f hurdle/chase winner Rathlin and bumper winner Grotesque, half-sister to bumper winner Baby Grey; trainer 0-39 in bumpers.
runners.comment: Third foal; half-brother to hurdle and chase winner The Grey Man; trainer 0-39 in bumpers.

runners.name: Teleleo
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam unraced close relation to 3m hurdle winner Akatara, half-sister to winners Aklan (1m4f Listed Flat/useful 2m hurdle), Anak (1m6f Flat/2m hurdle) and Akdam (1m4f Flat/2m-2m4f hurdle); trainer not noted for bumper winners, so perhaps best watched unless market vibes are strong.
runners.comment: Dam unraced close relation to a 3m hurdle winner; trainer not noted for bumper winners.

runners.name: Ultimately
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; dam maiden on Flat in Germany/Belgium/Netherlands, half-sister to winners Daytime Dreamer (1m3f-1m4f Flat/2m hurdle), Suivez L'Argent (5f 2yo) and Van Bom (Italian 7f); slipped up in a point bumper last month; trainer not known for bumper winners.
runners.comment: Out of half-sister to three Flat/one hurdle winners; trainer not known for bumper winners.

runners.name: Boy Oh Boy
runners.spotlight: 16-1 on debut, when tailed-off last of eight at Huntingdon (good) last month; tongue-tie goes on.
runners.comment: 16-1 when tailed-off last of eight on debut last month; tongue-tie goes on.
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Kenneth Sneath

races.race_name: Fen Collison-Hughes Happy 21st Birthday Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race) (Div I)

runners.name: Eustace Grenier
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in bumpers (placed on soft; matched that effort on good); 13-2 on last month's hurdles debut at Perth (2m, good) and although below the level of his bumper form (15l third), will doubtless benefit from that experience; player.
runners.comment: Showed promise in bumpers; below that level when third on hurdle debut but looks a player.

runners.name: No Risk Today
runners.spotlight: Won an Irish mares' maiden point on debut (good to yielding) last year; comfortably accounted for in two bumpers and a 2m hurdle for this trainer; others preferred.
runners.comment: Won an Irish mares' maiden point; held in two bumpers and a 2m hurdle for this trainer.

runners.name: Premier Tenor
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam French 2m1f hurdle winner, half-sister to French winners Premier Cru (2m2f-2m5f hurdle/chase) and Premiere Amie (2m1f/2m3f hurdle); won French 13.5f conditions event (good to soft) last October; hooded and tongue tied; interesting prospect on his first start for top trainer.
runners.comment: Dam French 2m1f hurdle winner; French 13.5f Flat winner; interesting on stable debut.

runners.name: Shamsat
runners.spotlight: French 1m3f Flat winner (heavy); 11-2 on debut for this trainer at Catterick (1m7f, soft) in January, when beaten about 6l in third; looked likely to come on for that experience and also when eventually going up in trip; can have a say here.
runners.comment: French 1m3f Flat winner (heavy); 6l third on British debut at Catterick; can have a say.

runners.name: L'Amalric
runners.spotlight: Failed to complete in four points but went off 9-2 on his debut in a Southwell bumper (good) in March, when he raced prominently and was beaten just over 2l; that was encouraging and a better starting point from which to begin in this sphere; no surprise if he is involved.
runners.comment: Encouraging bumper third latest; no surprise if he is involved on hurdles debut.

runners.name: Anzac Day
runners.spotlight: 1m2f Flat winner (soft) but has only made the track four times since that success for Andrew Balding in October 2023; has changed hands since beaten a distance at Ffos Las last September; looks best watched unless market vibes are strong.
runners.comment: 1m2f Flat winner; hasn't raced much since 2023; best watched unless market vibes strong.

runners.name: Thatsnotmyname
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to winners San Rumoldo (bumper/2m4f hurdle), En Joule (bumper) and Perculator (bumper and 2m5f/3m hurdle); opened 28-1 and backed into 9-2 for her debut, when beaten a long way out at Ludlow (good) last month; should benefit from that experience but has a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Well backed and well beaten on debut at Ludlow; should benefit from that but bit to prove.

runners.name: Hopjes
runners.spotlight: Placed once over 1m (AW) in the other code and 0-3 over hurdles; well beaten at Hereford latest; watch.
runners.comment: Moderate Flat performer who is 0-3 over hurdles; well beaten at Hereford latest; watch.

runners.name: Jacquette
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at big prices in all three starts.
runners.comment: Well beaten at big prices in all three starts.

runners.name: Teletime
runners.spotlight: Showed minor promise on debut at Taunton (good to soft) but took a backward step on his hurdles debut at Chepstow (2m, good); others preferred.
runners.comment: Took a backward step on his hurdles debut at Chepstow (2m, good); others preferred.

races.race_name: Fen Collison-Hughes Happy 21st Birthday Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race) (Div II)

runners.name: Crest Of Arms
runners.spotlight: Shaped okay in bumpers but appeared to go wrong when pulled up in such an event when 11-8 favourite at Taunton in March; made a promising start over hurdles when runner-up at Hexham (2m, good) 20 days ago and should not be far away.
runners.comment: Made a promising start over hurdles when runner-up at Hexham 20 days ago

runners.name: Sonyourastar
runners.spotlight: Made the frame in three Irish points; made a pleasing rules debut when fourth in a Market Rasen maiden (2m4f, good to soft) in March, but below that level when beaten 36l in a similar contest at Perth (2m, good to soft) eight days ago.
runners.comment: Fourth in a Market Rasen maiden in March, but below that level at Perth recently

runners.name: Kayla's Friend
runners.spotlight: Fair form in a couple of hurdles but was reported to have made a respiratory noise when sixth of seven finishers at Perth (2m4f, good) nine days ago; steps down in trip and should not be far away if all is well.
runners.comment: Reported to have made a respiratory noise when sixth at Perth nine days ago

runners.name: Blue Topaz
runners.spotlight: Point winner who landed a bumper at Sedgefield (2m1f, good) in October; pulled up on hurdles debut back there (2m4f, soft; 100-30) the following month when she was reported to have bled; has had a break since and may be worth another chance.
runners.comment: Reported to have bled when pulling up on hurdles debut at Sedgefield

runners.name: Just Call Me Buzz
runners.spotlight: Plodded on when staying on into third in a Chepstow maiden (2m, good) on his hurdles debut last month; may not come into his own until he steps up in trip or when he goes handicapping.
runners.comment: Stayed on into third in a Chepstow maiden on his hurdles debut last month

runners.name: Kind Intention
runners.spotlight: Definite promise in all three bumpers that were won by smart horses, including Storming George and Mydaddypaddy; makes hurdles debut but has been off since last April.
runners.comment: Definite promise in all three bumpers that were won by smart horses; hurdles debut

runners.name: Saint Arion
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in a point and beat only one home in a 20-runner bumper on his rules debut in March; makes hurdles debut but easy to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Pulled up in a point and beat only one home in a 20-runner bumper on his rules debut

runners.name: Pivotal Days
runners.spotlight: Won over 7f on the Flat for Ed Walker in 2024; has shown only modest form in two runs over hurdles for current yard.
runners.comment: Has shown only modest form in two runs over hurdles for current yard

runners.name: Medina Blue
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in a bumper in Ireland before finishing runner-up in two points; joined this yard for 19,000euros in April; sixth of seven finishers at Newton Abbot (2m1f, good) on hurdles debut nine days ago and unlikely to be seen to advantage until she is eligible for handicaps.
runners.comment: Sixth at Newton Abbot on hurdles debut nine days ago

runners.name: Royal Mark
runners.spotlight: Has just shown modest form in bumpers and over hurdles; first-time hood goes on.
runners.comment: Has just shown modest form in bumpers and over hurdles; first-time hood goes on

races.race_name: Step Into Luxury With Purple Granite Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Dearkeithandkaty
runners.spotlight: Consistent sort who got off the mark up in distance at Musselburgh (3m, good to soft) in February and has found one too good on all three occasions since, latest at Newton Abbot (3m2f, good) last month in first-time cheekpieces (retained); should be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Won at Musselburgh in February and has found one too good on all three occasions since.

runners.name: Betty's Tiara
runners.spotlight: Looked promising when winning a bumper on her debut in August 2023 but it took her an age to get going over hurdles, eventually winning a Uttoxeter maiden last September; hasn't gone on from that in three subsequent runs and returns following a break.
runners.comment: Won a Uttoxeter maiden last September; hasn't gone on from that in three subsequent runs

runners.name: Twist Of Fatecatch
runners.spotlight: Sole success came in a Fontwell maiden last April; has run with credit on several occasions since and shapes as though this step back up in distance will suit after he got outpaced when fourth at Wincanton (2m5f, good) last month; down to a career-low mark and should not be far away.
runners.comment: Shapes as though this step back up in distance will suit after he got outpaced last time.

runners.name: Lightfingered Jack
runners.spotlight: Placed twice in four starts over hurdles; steps up in trip in handicap debut and it would be no surprise if he shows significant improvement from a major yard.
runners.comment: Placed twice in four starts over hurdles; steps up in trip in handicap debut.

runners.name: Noble Blue
runners.spotlight: A prolific winner in points, he got off the mark under rules at Taunton (3m, good) last month; 5lb higher but looks the type that can go in again.
runners.comment: A prolific winner in points, he got off the mark under rules at Taunton last month.

runners.name: Gasmani
runners.spotlight: Point/bumper winner who showed improvement to get off the mark over hurdles at Taunton (3m, good) last month on his sixth attempt; creditable second over the same C&D subsequently and holds place claims at least while in this mood.
runners.comment: Showed improvement to get off the mark over hurdles at Taunton last month.

runners.name: Herbygoesbananas
runners.spotlight: Landed a bumper for Chris Gordon in November 2024; placed at Fontwell twice recently and should be suited by this step up in trip; not out of this.
runners.comment: Placed at Fontwell twice recently and should be suited by this step up in trip.

runners.name: I'm A Starman
runners.spotlight: Grand veteran with eight wins over hurdles, including two over C&D last summer; down to his last winning mark and should be sharper for his fifth at Hereford last month following a five-month layoff, but you have to think that one of his younger rivals should have his measure.
runners.comment: Should be sharper for his fifth at Hereford last month following a five-month layoff.

runners.name: Snowy Evening
runners.spotlight: Dual winner over fences at Doncaster on good; yet to win over hurdles and this is his first run in this sphere since March 2023; possibly better for this race before a summer campaign over fences.
runners.comment: Dual winner over fences at Doncaster on good; yet to win over hurdles.

runners.name: Blue In The West
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at the 22nd attempt in this sphere at Exeter (2m5f, good) last month when the pace collapse played into his hand; placed twice since but needs luck in running and his strike rate is off-putting.
runners.comment: Placed twice since winning at Exeter last month but needs luck in running.

runners.name: Dexperts Opinion
runners.spotlight: Showed modest form over hurdles and in a chase when trained in France; sold to join this yard for 13,000euros in November; a fascinating runner on his British debut and should be checked in the market.
runners.comment: Showed modest form over hurdles and in a chase when trained in France; 13,000euros buy

runners.name: Prince Imperial
runners.spotlight: Won four of his first five for Olly Murphy (including a handicap off 12lb higher) but looks to have lost his way, having been pulled up on his last three starts.
runners.comment: Looks to have lost his way, having been pulled up on his last three starts.

races.race_name: CopyBet Daily Profit Boost Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Ice Jet
runners.spotlight: Big improver who made it 2-3 in handicap hurdles when completing a double in smooth style at Huntingdon (2m4f, good to firm) on Tuesday; that was a three-runner race but he won with lots in hand and is 5lb well in under a double penalty here; leading contender.
runners.comment: Easy winner in last two starts and he's 5lb well in under a double penalty; big player

runners.name: Snapius
runners.spotlight: Won a similar handicap at Huntingdon (2m3f, good) in March and made another bold bid when runner-up at Ludlow (2m5f, good) 12 days ago; only just caught in that race and he's 4lb well in off the same mark here; strongly respected.
runners.comment: He was only just caught at Huntingdon and is 4lb well in off same mark here; respected

runners.name: The Great Escape
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on handicap debut over C&D (good) last July but he's struggled since, including when pulled up on chase debut at Wetherby in March; overall record is now 0-9 and he has plenty to prove after a short break; cheekpieces worn last time are removed.
runners.comment: Nine-race maiden who has struggled over hurdles/fences in last four runs; lots to prove

runners.name: Stiletto
runners.spotlight: Versatile sort who won three times (2m2f-3m) in June-September last year; only 1lb higher than for his last success but he's been well held in his last three runs and needs a major revival after wind surgery.
runners.comment: 8yo who has been well held in last three runs and needs a major revival after wind surgery

runners.name: Kylenoe Dancer
runners.spotlight: Has won a handicap (off 6lb lower) but there are long absences in her profile and she was pulled up when returning from one at Fontwell in March.
runners.comment: Has some long absences in her profile and was pulled when returning from one in March

runners.name: Estacas
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on old form for David Bridgwater but he returned for new yard after a mammoth absence with a well-held eighth at Stratford (2m2f, good) last month; shaped as though he needed that run but this looks a tough assignment against some improvers and he's probably best watched; cheekpieces (tried once in 2023) are reapplied.
runners.comment: Back from a mammoth absence with a well-held eighth at Stratford; passed over

runners.name: Butchers Bill
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish maiden who has failed to complete over fences (fell/pulled up) in the last two of his three runs for current yard; overall record is now 0-11 and he's not finished closer than 10l to a winner; opposable.
runners.comment: 11-race maiden who has failed to complete over fences in last two starts; opposable

races.race_name: Read Johnny Burke's Blog At CopyBet Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Playful Fox
runners.spotlight: 0-4 but was in the process of running his best race to date when unseating two out at Uttoxeter (2m, good) 20 days ago when coming to challenge; still unexposed and should go close, at the least.
runners.comment: Running his best race so far when unseating two out at Uttoxeter 20 days ago.

runners.name: Saccary
runners.spotlight: Yet to win over hurdles but has filled the runner-up berth on his last three starts, latest when beaten a neck at Uttoxeter (2m, good) 20 days ago; looks sure to be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Yet to win over hurdles but has filled the runner-up berth on his last three starts.

runners.name: Ballylicky Bay
runners.spotlight: Point/bumper winner; hasn't got going in four runs over hurdles, latest when last of five at Fontwell (2m1f, good to soft) last month on handicap debut, still early days but she needs to show improvement.
runners.comment: Hasn't got going in four runs over hurdles, latest when last of five at Fontwell.

runners.name: Aquifolia
runners.spotlight: 0-17 under rules and was brought down early on at Taunton (2m, good) last month; has been placed a couple of times but is hard to fancy.
runners.comment: 0-17 under rules and was brought down early on at Taunton last month.

runners.name: We Ain't Walking
runners.spotlight: Hasn't lasted home in an Irish point or any hurdles outing; returns from 592 days off and makes stable debut for in-form trainer; any market move should be noted.
runners.comment: Hasn't lasted home in an Irish point or any hurdles outing; returns from 592 days off.

runners.name: Fond Farewell
runners.spotlight: Yet to get off the mark over hurdles but unseated two out when travelling easily at Huntingdon (2m, good) in March; pulled up at Uttoxeter since but could be dangerous off bottom weight.
runners.comment: Unseated two out when travelling easily at Huntingdon in March; pulled up since.

runners.name: East End Girl
runners.spotlight: Dual winner over hurdles but last win was back in October 2023; better could have been expected when fifth of eight at Hereford (2m, good) in March; has dropped to a career-low mark but doesn't appeal for win purposes.
runners.comment: Better could have been expected when fifth at Hereford in March.

runners.name: Dynamite Defense
runners.spotlight: Won over 1m on the Flat in Ireland in 2024, but Olly Murphy was unable to get a win out of him in five runs over hurdles and two on the Flat; sold for 5,500gns to join new yard last month; best watched on stable debut.
runners.comment: Won over 1m on the Flat in Ireland in 2024; no joy since and makes debut for new yard.
